date: 2022-12-15
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Invoices
msg_id: 18513467d532366e
---

Hi Brian,

Thanks for this, and was great to meet in person last month.

Is it okay to make payment in early Jan? I'm planning to withdraw a portion
of funds from the trust from my grandmother, and was planning to do it
early 2023, at which point I can make the full payment then. I'm a little
cash strapped through the end of the year after paying off 2020 and 2021 US
tax payments in the past few months.

I'll follow up in another thread in the next week or two re: topics we
discussed when we met in person.

Thanks!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-12-15
to: John Montag <johnmontag@montagwealth.com>
subject: Re: CRYPTO
msg_id: 18513447e566f205
---

Hi John and Ben,

Sorry for my delay in following up here. I guess taking a step back, this
are some of the bigger pictures I'm seeing:

-1) I need to withdraw probably somewhere around $30k in early 2023 to pay
for some taxes/accounting services, fund some travel, and likely buy a new
laptop/phone.

-2) the grayscale ETF seems on some level very attractive right now with
its 40%+ discount. As does Coinbase considering they seem to be
weathering the storm in the crypto ecosystem quite well.

-3) as always, the macro picture is not my place of enterprise. It seems
interest rates are a bit of a lagging indicator to the market, and I know
they were raised again yesterday or today, although to a lesser extent than
the last hikes. I'm not sure if the picture is for more hikes in the future
or we might be near the bottom of the market. I guess another piece of the
macro picture that would be helpful is to know what is the current
allocation of funds in the trust. If it's under 25% or so in GBTC/Coinbase,
I think I'd want to start increasing that soon. If it's more like 50%,
maybe we hold off for now. Either way, it seems like a good idea to deploy
more slowly than we have ben, so maybe something more like $500 into each
Coinbase and GBTC per month? Very open to suggestions around this.

-4) it sounds like i'll likely be receiving some inheritance from my
paternal grandmother (maybe 100-150k) in the coming year or so which I'll
probably look to deposit half of that into the trust if that is possible.
Or if I should be looking at instruments such as IRAs/etc, let me know (or
if this is better talked through with my accountant).

-5) in terms of tax on withdrawls from the trust, it sounds like according
to my accountant that there is only tax to be paid on returns. I'm not sure
how to best manage this, but is there some sort of annual statement I can
provide him for the upcoming 2021 filing outlining trust withdrawals and
performance?

Let me know if there's anything else, or what you think re; the above.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-12-15
to: Harald Eisenhauer <harald@deep-ecosystems.com>
subject: Re: Mentoring for the DEEP Startup Ecosystem Accelerator
msg_id: 185133728bf5cb1d
---

Hi Harald,

Very sorry for the delay on this - I've had an absolute whirlwind month or
two of travel, holidays, and work. More than happy to help however I can,
and more than happy to help broadcast on social in the future. Let me know
when I can help with mentoring or anything else :)

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-12-14
to: Kevin Gardner <kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Update/Catching up on payments
msg_id: 18512d7d4c9fef0f
---

Hi Kevin,

Very sorry for my delay on this - I've been quite swamped with work, as
well as dealing with a number of headaches re: onshoring my finances in the
US and the markets in general (I've taken a pretty major hit in a few
portfolios I have, as well as had my metamask wallet for Vang Capital
hacked and lost some funds in the FTX collapse (although certainly was not
as impacted as I could have been).

I know I have a standing balance somewhere between £3-5k and you had
offered to set up a payment plan to address this. If it's okay, I'm
planning to liquidate some funds in a US investment account I have at the
beginning on 2023, so should be able to make payment to you in full by the
second week of January at the latest (hopefully the first). Could you
provide me an pair of invoices (one for Vang Capital, one for Vang
Advisory), that I can use to make a single payment to catch up the accounts
then?

I also know I have quite a bit of accounts with HMRC (they owe me
self-assessment refund from last year, I owe them a few periods of VAT and
potentially business income tax, and we have the upcoming self-assessment
filing which maybe there will be a refund (or balance) from. I am planning
to pay these off along with the liquidations I'm making in very early 2023.
I know I also still need to dive into my bank statements and double check
what I believe was a double payment of a payment plan of my Corporate Tax
from 2020, which I will look to do in the next week or two. If there is
anywhere I can access a summary of all of these balances, that would
certainly be helpful.

As a reminder, I've basically stopped all activity on Vang Advisory, as I
now have a US entity set up for it, and will hope to wind this down as soon
as possible in 2023 once I have made pending payments on account with HMRC.

Let me know if there's anything I'm forgetting, and as always thanks for
your help.

Best,

Matthew

subject: Re: Head of Growth at Structure.fi, the Future of Global Finance

---

Hi Alina,
Thanks for reaching out.
Could you tell me a bit about what differentiates Structure vs Robinhood, eToro,
or other low fee centralized stock/crypto trading apps, or multi-function
personal finance apps like Revolut or Cashapp? i'm  hoping for something more
strategic/defensible/meaningful than "available in alternative/additional
markets" or micro features like the ability to trade directly between stocks and
crypto - something more in the vein of. a vision/mission/roadmap.
I'm pretty happy in my current role and paid quite well, but could maybe be
interested.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-12-04
to: Sam Cobbina <Sam@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Self Assessment Questionnaire for Tax Year ending 5th April 2022
msg_id: 184df365dcfbd608
---

Hi Sam,
Sorry for the delay on this - it seems that my previous employer switched a
bunch of their HR systems, and it ended up taking about 8-10 emails to get them
to send me P45/60/etc.
Please find attached the questionnaire (i used  last years as the attachment on
this thread didn't seem to work for me - let me know if you need any additional
information).
i assume  you can fill in the income from Vang Advisory (although there were
likely quite a bit of directors loans  that were funneled into Vang Advisory to
cover tax payments), but let me know if you need anything additionally from me.
Also, i should  have about a £3.5k refund pending on my personal tax account -
HMRC never sent a direct deposit, and when I called them they said they needed
to change and process my address change, and i haven't  had a chance to call
them back since to process the actual payment.
Thanks!
Matthew

---
date: 2022-12-03
to: matthew.eisner@samba.tv
subject: Add to deck Qs to be answered
msg_id: 184d97bd5d479587
---

How will we position relationship between samba and protocol?

---
date: 2022-11-24
to: Mauvis Ledford <mauvis@bitninja.sg>
subject: Re: Intro: Matt & Mauvis
msg_id: 184a80523a32f7f6
---

Hi Mauvis,

I’m terribly sorry, I had a hectic day of travel including a flight delay
and then got wrapped up in catching up with family tonight. Was probably
not the best idea on my end to book our call for a day I had to travel, but
was keen for us to get a chance to chat.

If it’s okay, I’ll book something for next week?

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-11-22
to: Ben Harris <benharris@montagwealth.com>
subject: Re: CRYPTO
msg_id: 1849fe12a4f2dbc7
---

Hi Ben,

My impression is that GBTC would likely be sold off to another more
traditional finance company were the company currently managing it (DCG)
we’re to crash. But I do think slowing down is maybe a good idea. Could we
start doing $500 per week, as well as $100 or so per week in Coinbase (who
seem to have come out of the FTX debacle looking quite good).

Open to thoughts as well on this, particularly if you think the bottom of
the market might be a ways off.

Thanks for reaching out,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-11-21
to: Christine Leuthold <ce3136@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Maybe you can walk me through this?
msg_id: 184979c442dbced7
---

I fly back to LA Dec 8 but will probably go to SF and potentially Taipei
for work. Have a flight to Bogotá on Jan 15 but no solid plans for the 5 or
so weeks in between

Matthew

---
date: 2022-11-06
to: Christine Chang <christine@alumni.ie.edu>
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 1844e9dc8315730f
---

Hi Christine,

Hope all is well with you!

I just wanted to drop you a note as I’ll be in CDMX most of the next 2
weeks. Would love to grab lunch/a coffee if you’re around and not too busy
:)

Let me know if there are some times that might work for you.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-11-04
to: Rizwan Noor <rizwan@algorandaccelerate.com>
subject: Re: Workshop: KPI/Success Measurement
msg_id: 1844125f201a43c2
---

The Wednesday and Friday both work well for me :)



On Thu, 3 Nov 2022 at 10:35, Rizwan Noor <rizwan@algorandaccelerate.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2022-11-04
to: Colin Allison <colin.allison92@gmail.com>, Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Yohann/Colin intro
msg_id: 184412271d2a8466
---

Hi Yohann,

Please meet Colin, who I’m working with on setting up a web3 venture
studio/accelerator program. He’s potentially going to be syndicating a few
angel deals to bootstrap a fund we’re raising, and thought your platform
might be useful in helping with that.

Colin, please meet Yohann, who is the the founder and CEO of
AngelsPartners, who I’ve been advising for a while. He has a very extensive
database of angels, and in general is working on ways to scale revenue and
build some success stories as he tries to grow AP further.

There are a number of areas of overlap I could see, so hopefully you two
can find a time to connect :)

Matthew

---
date: 2022-11-01
to: Sam Cobbina <Sam@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: VAT Due
msg_id: 1843428a31c29a6c
---

Hii Sam,
Sorry for the delay - I do not have any invoices or expenses from this period,
as I've basically wound down activity within Vang Advisory.
Let me know if you need any more information.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2022-10-26
to: Tim Sandwisch <tim@webblen.com>
subject: Re: Webblen Executive Team Adjustments & Updated Pitch Deck
msg_id: 1841601817089f51
---

Hey Tim,

Sorry I haven’t been able to make the call in a couple of weeks - it’s been
a crazy stint of travel and getting our product prepped to launch at Samba.
Appreciate the update, and planning to join the call next week.

I’ll also try and get you some feedback on the deck by EOD tomorrow.

Thanks!

Matthew

---

date: 2022-10-14
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Yohann/Alex intro
msg_id: 183d65e32f54b110
---

Hmm, I’m not sure, last he told me he was looking for something new. I’d
ping him one more time and leave it at that.

Pelin had a busy week last week, so had asked if I could introduce you two
next week, so that’s in the pipeline



On Fri, 14 Oct 2022 at 11:15, Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2022-10-13
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Vang Advisory filings
msg_id: 183d2ace80a0d284
---

Thanks - i've esigned  the docs and made payment via credit card/ACH.
When you have some time, it would be good to discuss 1) getting Vang Advisory
LLC up and running in the US, 2) planning to transition Vang Capital to the US,
and 3) some wider tax questions around.my employment, residency, and trust i
inherited from my grandmother looking forward.
I'll actually be in Sarasota the week of Oct 24 if it might make sense to meet
in person there.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-10-11
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Vang Advisory filings
msg_id: 183c723b16800aff
---

Okay thanks - is there anything else you need from me to file? And I assume
I can just pay that through the IRS web portal - any references I need to
include in that?

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-10-10
to: Alexander Potter <alex@coglabs.io>, Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Yohann/Alex intro
msg_id: 183c3660f689cb3c
---

Hi Yohann and Alex - I’ve mentioned you both to each other so will keep
this short. Alex, Yohann is building an angel/startup matchmaking platform
that I advise that’s seen some solid growth, but that needs to figure out
the next stages. Yohann, Alex has a long history supporting founders,
providing deal flow to investors, and growing businesses/prepping them to
raise funds.

I’ll let you two take it from here!

Matthew

date: 2022-10-09
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>, nick@gringaming.com
subject: Nick/Yohann intro
msg_id: 183be5ecd19053c4
---

Hi Nick,

Please meet Yohann, a serial entrepreneur who has been growing
AngelsPartners, which I do some advisory for. They help startups find angel
funding. His previous business was around predictive analytics related to
crypto token prices, which might be interesting for you to chat on as well.

Yohann, please meet Nick, who I mentioned earlier runs a gaming studio and
crypto/gaming payments sdk is having a bit of trouble raising some funds to
bridge them to their A round. Maybe something AngelsPartners could help
with?

Hope you two can find time to connect, will let yall take it from here.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2022-09-30
to: "Arnold, Alex" <Alex.Arnold@truist.com>
subject: Re: Fraud prevention call
msg_id: 1838efafa37dd3f8
---

I wasn’t able to get through to the point where it listed the transactions,
but I believe it would have been $5.11 to FIFA Connect (there were quite a
few of these charges, basically a raffle/collectible purchase). This would
have been late last night or early this morning ET.

Thanks for you help!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-09-30
to: "Arnold, Alex" <Alex.Arnold@truist.com>
subject: Fraud prevention call
msg_id: 1838eeea43561483
---

Hi Alex,
I am currently in Portugal (and will be traveling extensively in Europe, South
America, and Asia the next 6-9 months), and have been having my card flagged by
the fraud prevention line.
Last night I called in and was able to clear the transactions. Today it seems
that on an international line, the automated call cannot detect me entering my
zip code, and won't allow me to complete the verification.
Could you please ensure there are no freezes on my card and it stopped getting
flagged for these transactions?
Thanks,
Matthew

Matthew

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-09-29
to: Jeng Yang Chia <chia@panteracapital.com>
subject: Another potential startup - Webblen
msg_id: 18389807a94183c2
---

Hi Chia,

Another startup that might be interesting for you raising seed round - I've
advised them for a few years, and they've picked up quite a bit of grant
funding in the past. Deck linked below, let me know if you might want an
intro :)

Hi,
Figured I'd put all of this in an email;
1) I'm going to be getting to Sarasota tomorrow (or I guess technically today
local time) - anything i need to do? Does the guard know to let me in? I also
have a few credit cards that should have arrived that I need if you've seen
them?
2) Loan til end of month - i currently  have a negative balance on my Truist
account, and was wondering if you could send me $1000 that i can  send back at
my next paycheck at the end of the month. I'm still catching up on some tax and
other payments and have taken a hit on stocks and crypto recently, but have a
transfer from my stock account on its way, but it will take a few days
3) Vang Capital loan repayment - I've attached the doc signed on my side, needs
to be signed on yours with a witness
4) Vang Capital promissory note - I found the old one that I think Zach wrote
for you, and Brian approved. I'm just going to copy and edit it, but I still
need to chat with Brian on if it's best to do that through the UK company or go
ahead and set up a US one for Vang Capital.

Let me know if there's anything else.
Matthew

Second, I did not realize there was a standing balance. I will call to set up a

on the 2021 tab of the last screenshot) an extra payment I made of about £1400
towards what was supposed to be my final payment of a payment plan I had set up
- at the time, the initial direct deposit failed, i called in to pay it, and
then they charged me the direct deposit anyways I believe. I believe this was
towards my 2020 company tax liability, i will dig through my records tonight and
see if I can pinpoint the date).
I do also want to flag that I want to make sure we're 1) looking to wind down
Vang Advisory in the near future, 2) ready for filings related to Vang Capital
(as well as personal filings for UK tax), which I know blindsided Antonio a bit
in terms of their complexity, and 3) i know i have some balances on account with

single payment and catch up on those?

Matthew

---
date: 2022-09-13
to: Jeng Yang Chia <jengyang.chia@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: Grin Gaming Investment Opportunity
msg_id: 183386831edf75e1
---

Hey Chia,

Hope all is well! I have a friend who runs Grin Gaming/SMILE Coing (a web3
gaming studio and payment rails/SDK) who asked me if I knew anyone at
Pantera, and you jumped to mind. I've attached a bit of their info below,
but more than happy to make an intro if you're interested. They're
currently seeing some very nice revenue growth and looking to raise a bit
of funding to start scaling up some of their initiatives.

And more than happy to continue sending you stuff like this if it's useful
- just let me know.

Also, I'm in SF for the next 4-5 days - would love to grab a coffee if
you're around!

Best,

Matthew


---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Nick Bucheleres <nick@gringaming.com>
Date: Wed, Aug 24, 2022 at 8:06 PM
Subject: Grin Gaming Investment Opportunity
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>


Hi Matt, we are looking to fill the final $500k distribution of our seed
extension at Grin Gaming <https://gringaming.com>.  We're having a super
exciting summer, where we've seen our revenue grow by ~100% m/m for the
last 5 months.  Our revenue is made up of USD and crypto, and the crypto
winter put us on our heels, hence the re-opening of our seed round (which
will be led by BAM Ventures).  That said, we've been focusing more heavily
on USD growth the last few months, which is working: we are now banking
$70k+ in cash revenue per month, but this is still below our $200,000/mo
burn.



You can find our full investor dashboard here
<https://analytics.amplitude.com/share/b040145980124ddb8da9a825489fc320>.

We're very excited to get into the NFL season, which is historically the
biggest revenue season of the year for us.  We also have a soccer product
ready to go for the World Cup & MLS, and as such we're expecting our
revenue doubling trend to continue well through the rest of the year.

The valuation for the seed extension round is $13M pre/$16M post, which is
a screaming deal for investors, as we're currently in talks with Series A
investors for a $10M round with post-money valuation between $40-70M.

Take a look our info and let me know if you think anyone you know might be
a good fit for this pocket deal.  Talk soon,


*Nick Bucheleres*
CEO
630.247.7010
*gringaming.com <http://gringaminginc.com>*


Sent via Superhuman <https://sprh.mn/?vip=nick@gringaming.com>

---

date: 2022-09-09
to: Jonathan Saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>, matthew.eisner@samba.tv
subject: Following up
msg_id: 1832021e064204a6
---

Hey Jon,

Was great to catch up today and excited to potentially get to work together
again soon. Following up on a few points from our call:

Could you please provide me 1) your day rate and 2) vague/broad time
estimates (not to be held as rigid/any sort of guarantee) for the following
design work so we can get a ballpark of what we might be looking at from a
cost standpoint:

   - a) brand identity and basic logos, icons, colours, fonts, slide deck,
   layouts, and other assets
   - b) 5 page website (ideally in Webflow)
   - c) Mobile UI/Designs for about 20-30 app screens
   - d) Marketing collateral for Beta launch


Please find linked various resources that might be useful

   - Our final landing pages (built by my creative director friend):
      - https://app.samba.tv/01-myd
      - https://app.samba.tv/02-wte
      - https://app.samba.tv/03-collectible
      - https://app.samba.tv/tv-ecomm


   - Our final ad creative (made by my creative director friend):
   https://www.figma.com/file/lSHeuErYnk099CDocZspJg/DB-Figma-Notebook?node-id=161%3A184


   - Initial design work by iLabs (we rejected but iterated from):
   https://www.figma.com/file/mhxSlzYB8yCvTO3qVAAXzV/Creatives-%E2%80%A2-Samba-x-iLabs?node-id=0%3A1


   - My app UI mockups:
   https://www.figma.com/file/qhtdvkeVbbphBpp3FUhoOI/Farscape-App-Prototypes?node-id=0%3A1


   - My initial creative brief for our design agency (was more focused on
   Phase 1 of getting assets for Ads and Landing Pages for USP Validation
   Tests, which is done - second half of the doc has ideas for continuing
   scope though):
   https://docs.google.com/document/d/1pGZydaA6bAEdQwF71uQwrnDlG-NB8-TTAopBm-UXReA/edit?usp=sharing



I’ll also collect some of our inspo/mood board for you over the coming days
and send along to you.

Let me know if you have any thoughts/questions or think of something I’m
forgetting!

Best,

Matthew

As mentioned, please meet Qomoo, who is currently the CRO at Webblen, the Head

funds. I certainly think he'd be a great mentor for the upcoming Algorand
accelerator, as well as generally potentially beneficial for you to connect
with.

investors in Algorand and are quite embedded in the ecosystem, as well as a

---
date: 2022-09-08
to: Pat Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
subject: Finally following up
msg_id: 1831ef0d5bc91972
---

Hi Pat,
Hope all is well with you, and apologies for my delay in following up. I hit a
bit of a hectic stint between work, travel, family, and a couple of deals I was
working on.
I took a look through some of the articles on the Variant Perception blog, and
there are two contexts I could see talking about them in 1) the analysis
themselves, and 2) how you're using this content to drive business.
On point 1) - this is certainly much more macro than I'm used to as of late, but
is reminiscent of some of the work I did in college trying to do quant analysis
to predict/understand iPhone adoption based on factors such as demographics,
network provider, etc. or some of the sentiment analysis work i did trying to
measure impact of news stories on stock prices and brand perception for a
company called Prime Research. Most of the data sources you're working with I'm
unfamiliar with, but I can understand most of them well enough when linked to
commodities and logical knock on effects to related industries. I also imagine
there is potentially some more micro data you could include here depending on
the industry at play (maybe consumer focus groups/subject pools for industries
with heavy consumer demand effects, supply chain data from Flexport or Vortexa,
or even via partnerships, etc. (and I also imagine quite a few blockchain based
solutions such as OCEAN Protocol). in general though, i think quite a few of
these points of analysis are similar to how I and most people i know look at
crypto (although I probably lean a bit to micro just given my background and
don't play the wider market swings that well) - but crypto generally is heavily
influenced by wider market, with exceptions often resulting from predictable (or
unpredictable) spikes in certain areas of the market - Ethereum should be very
interesting over the coming week with its merge on the 15th - I will likely sell
half of my non-chain based holdings around the 14th or just before the merge
under the assumption the price will drop post-run-up-speculation). I plan to
keep my on chain  holdings (and have been flipping as much back to eth as
possible from atl coins) in hopes there will be a new token created on the fork 
i will  be eligible to collect (and likely dump quite quickly).
On point 2) - I imagine maybe the dashboard is more central as a customer
engagement point? But in general these blog posts seem to be chocked full with
quite interesting insight, which I imagine could be relatively easily repurposed
into more client/biz dev facing marketing materials, or even facilitate cross
promotion through data partners. Happy to chat through this a bit, and might
have some friends who could help you if that's of interest.

Also in general, I'd love access to the research dashboard portal to poke around
in there. I imagine that's where much of the data viz for the blog analysis is
being generated unless I'm misunderstanding the portal.
Also, i wanted  to raise a couple of potentially interesting connections - let
me know if you might like intros to any of them! * Idan - working on an ML/AI/Marketplace to provide game testing to studios,
   quite a stacked early client. He was founder of the first company I worked
   with out of school. does technical DD for a16z, previously head of ML at
   Unity

 * Nick - friend from college who runs Grin Gaming (prediction market based web3
   game studio) and SMILE Coin (SDK and payments rails for crypto gaming
   companies)

 * Qomoo & Byowave  - generally a supernetworker, advisor for a company that is
   about to receive significant funding from microsoft for modular gaming
   controllers designed to improve accessibility for disabled gamers (and
   generally provide customizable controller setups) that I thought might be
   interesting to you


I'm actually in SF next week if you happen to be around - or would love to catch
up via video/phone call soon. And as always, happy to help with anything early
stage/crypto/consumer/etc oriented :)
Best,
Matthew

my thoughts are:
i'm  a little confused as to where this sits between aggregators/directories
like weedmaps and eaze and dispensary POS systems like blaze (or maybe rather,
how this will compete/be more attractive than). i have  a friend who was working
on something similar to this that was trying to give dispensaries more control
over their customer relationships (tools for text, email, contests, discounts),
facilitate online ordering, etc. He wasn't the right tech person to get it off
the ground, but i know  he also had a lot of issues with payment rails/banking,
and the value of vs work to service dispensaries. NE market is an interesting
focus. would maybe consider investing if min ticket wasn't 10x my max ticket,
although would ideally prefer there to be some traction/proof of concept/pilot
deals secured

date: 2022-09-06
to: Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>
subject: Following up (Grin/Webblen/Oasis/Caduceus)
msg_id: 183145c4eafb4532
---

Hey man,

Was good to catch up last week and excited to be helping with the Algorand
accelerator again.

Following up on a few threads from our call and otherwise:

   - Webblen and Grin - both raising, I've included intros below, happy to
   intro you to Kai <https://www.linkedin.com/in/kaiss/> (CEO of Webblen)
   or Nick <https://www.linkedin.com/in/nickeleres/> (CEO/CTO of Grin/SMILE)


   - Qomoo <https://www.linkedin.com/in/qomooatomsa/> - CRO for Webblen,
   Head of Growth for Gin (so could be a good way to get a brief overview on
   both), also super networked in general and might be worth connecting with
   around the Algorand accelerator to drive some referrals/potentially bring
   on as an advisor


   - Oasis accelerator: I forgot to bring up our last call that I believe
   we still have the second payment for the Oasis accelerator due. I'll go
   back and review our agreement and see what we had laid out (I remember
   there was a variable piece based on startup success) and summarize what was
   there so we can discuss.


   - Caduceus accelerator/ecosystem programs - I was connected to the
   incoming Program Director for the Caduceus accelerator by Laurence from
   TechTree, which seem to be a super early stage L1. I'm talking with them
   about doing some light advisory, but he was keen to connect with you as
   well. They're at a stage where they don't seem to really have a clear
   strategy in place, so might be interesting to get involved if the chain is
   actually interesting (but not sure it is).


Let me know if there's anything else from your side.

Best,

Matthew




*Webblen*

Today’s consumer technology and most popular business models have had a
negative effect on human behavior and our communities’ well-being. Social
media and other ‘engagement’ platforms have left our communities polarized,
lonely, and disengaged. This hurts our culture, businesses, and mental
health. Webblen INC seeks to realize an economy that incentivizes
purpose-driven businesses, solutions, and social behavior.

Being a B2B2C platform, we leverage business tech, consumer tech, and
cryptocurrencies to realize our vision.

With Webblen’s platform, investors, grantors,  partners, and clients have
the opportunity to directly influence how communities grow and behave
across the globe.

Webblen INC seeks to raise $2M at a post-money valuation of $8M.
Stakeholders that allocate capital to the company’s raise receive both
equity and a denomination of Webblen tokens (“WBLN”). WBLN tokens are
distributed from Webblen’s token treasury. The amount distributed from the
treasure will reflect their equity ownership in Webblen INC. These tokens
can immediatley be liquidated for cash or utilized to influence the
behavior of a community of their choosing.





*Grin Gaming*

we are looking to fill  a final $500k-$1M distribution for our seed
extension at Grin Gaming <https://gringaming.com/>.  We've had an exciting
Q2/3, wherein we've seen our revenue grow by ~100% m/m for the last 5
months.  Our revenues are realized through both Web2 and Web3 payment
rails, and despite the crypto winter we are taking home $4k/day in net
revenue.  Based on our revenue growth we will be EBITA break-even  by the
end of Q4.

[image: Screen Shot 2022-09-06 at 12.50.53 PM.png]

You can find our full investor dashboard here
<https://analytics.amplitude.com/share/b040145980124ddb8da9a825489fc320>.

We're very excited to get into the NFL season, which is historically the
biggest revenue season of the year for us.  We also have a soccer product
ready to go for the World Cup & MLS, and as such we're expecting our
revenue doubling trend to continue well through the rest of the year.

The valuation for the seed extension round is $13M pre/$16M post, which is
a screaming deal for investors, as we're currently in talks with Series A
investors for a $10M round with post-money valuation between $40-70M.

I just wanted to follow up on this and see if we had started to shift some
of my portfolio allocation into the GBTC trust, and if not if we could
start (or potentially increase the rate of buying slightly, given the price
of Bitcoin dropping (I would be surprised if it goes under $16000, although
don’t necessarily think it’s hit it’s bottom yet).

I also got hit with a relatively sizeable tax bill for last year, and
wanted to withdraw another $10k from the trust to my personal account. This
will be the last one until next year at the earliest, and hopefully a quite
a bit longer than that.

Let me know if there are any issues with either of these.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2022-08-27
to: Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>
subject: Re: New Algorand Accelerator
msg_id: 182de43d9b9c6bf2
---

Sorry for the delay - hectic week of travel. How’s this coming Tuesday at
4pm or Thursday afternoon anytime EST for you?

---
date: 2022-08-25
to: Guillaume Agis <guillaume.agis@gmail.com>
subject: Re: React native devs/contractor
msg_id: 182d6fab8c4125fa
---

There might be some overlap with what we’re building there. How are you
managing the credit card processing out of curiosity?

Other agency is called iLabs, we’ve been using them more for design stuff
up til now but they can do react, so I think Brandon is talking to them
next week about that



On Thu, 25 Aug 2022 at 14:44, Guillaume Agis <guillaume.agis@gmail.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2022-08-25
to: Brandon Ooi <brandon.ooi@samba.tv>, Guillaume Agis <guillaume.agis@gmail.com>
subject: Brandon/Guillaume Intro
msg_id: 182d6f95e702e9f3
---

Hi Brandon,

Please meet Guillaume (based in France), who I mentioned is a friend and
former colleague of mine now running a development agency in France. It
sounds like they have a bit of a full pipeline for the next 6 weeks or so,
but I thought it’d be good for you to connect and see if if they might be a
good fit for what we’re looking for.

Guillaume, please meet Brandon (based in California), who is leading our
engineering for what we’re working on at Samba (basically bringing our
first major consumer-facing product to market). We’ve had a bit of trouble
finding the devs we need, particularly front end/react. Also I think you’ll
find what we’re working on interesting given some of your focuses as of
late.

I’ll let you two take it from here!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-08-25
to: Guillaume Agis <guillaume.agis@gmail.com>
subject: Re: React native devs/contractor
msg_id: 182d6f4478b5f8ea
---

That’s good timing - the other agency were talking to would be October
start. There’s also a 1/4 chance we’re building an NFT marketplace, so
maybe some more full stack work as well.

I’ll intro you to my tech partner shortly :)



On Thu, 25 Aug 2022 at 14:40, Guillaume Agis <guillaume.agis@gmail.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2022-08-23
to: Guillaume Agis <guillaume.agis@gmail.com>
subject: React native devs/contractor
msg_id: 182ccd463e709429
---

Hey man,

Do you have or know anyone who could help my company Samba with a react
native project? We need them to have a company for tax purposes, but
thought you might have some people on your team who could potentially help.

Let me know if that’s interesting to you or you know anyone. They’d be
working with my tech partner who was the CTO of CrunchyRoll, and he’s keen
to start talking to people ASAP.

Hope all is well!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-08-23
to: Valerie Velvette <xxvalerievelvette@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Contact form reply
msg_id: 182cc7abb2c5471c
---

Hi - I’m so sorry, I just found out I need to fly out last minute for work
tonight.

Please don’t worry about the deposit if you aren’t able to find someone
else for that time, and hopefully we can link up next time I’m in LA in a
month or so


On Tue, 23 Aug 2022 at 10:38, Valerie Velvette <xxvalerievelvette@gmail.com>
wrote:

date: 2022-08-23
to: Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>
subject: Re: New Algorand Accelerator
msg_id: 182cbdd28e034300
---

All going well so far :) hope the same for you as well.

Congrats re the Algorand accelerator. Would of course love to help as
partner, mentor, or otherwise.

Does it make sense to jump on a call in the next few weeks to discuss a bit
more? Would also love to pick your brain a bit on what I’m working on.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2022-08-15
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: 2021 Tax info
msg_id: 182a3de8a69a3512
---

Hi Brian,

Sorry for the delay - I've been in a hectic period of travel.

Please find linked my responses to the question form -
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1D02miFZklk7OT6TTgZ2MBAFJFr2lTG8lIXUUB1OUrnc/edit?usp=sharing

I haven't gotten a few pieces of info regarding Vang Advisory, but
hopefully maybe there is enough info via Xero? Let me know if not and i can
chase my UK accountants.


Also, I realized I don't have my 1099 from 2020, for which I still need to
make payment, and it seems to have disappeared from the document portal.
Could you send another copy of this along?


Lastly, I have my US company Vang Advisory LLC,which I am not sure if I
need to make any filings for as it has basically been inactive apart from
incorporation via Stripe Atlas in Delaware and the annual renewal. I have a
bank account but have received and spent no money. I will no longer use my
UK company going forward, so it would be good to discuss how we'll manage
this moving forward/what you need me to keep track of, and I also wanted to
check that there haven't been filings we've needed to be making (and I'm
sure will need to once the company becomes active in terms of receiving
revenue and spending money).


Let me know if there's anything else or you have any questions on the above!

Thanks!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-08-06
to: Matty@open3.com
subject: Following up from IG
msg_id: 18274b39e8fc0333
---

Great to finally connect :) I’ve been a big fan since I discovered your
dipped paintings probably 5+ years ago at least (maybe even more like 10).

As mentioned, I’d love to chat about how I might help with what you’re
building at Open3. I’ve worked launching and managing accelerators and
startup programs for the better part of the past 5 years (launched 20
programs with Startupbootcamp, consulted and advised various other programs
in their setups, and mentored for seedstars, Google launchpad, various
university programs, etc).

I’ve been playing with an idea I’ll likely launch in a few years that
sounds somewhat similar/like a combination of what you’re doing with the
art town + open3, but more geared to tech and more rural. I’d love to chat
and potentially help out with what you’re doing, in hopes that it might
help steer/provide learnings what I’m looking to do, as well as just to
contribute to build something cool and creative :)

I’m quite flexible Monday/Tuesday or Friday of next week if there might be
some times that work for you for a quick intro - just let me know when
might be ideal!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-08-05
to: Kai <mukaiss@webblen.com>
subject: Catch up next week
msg_id: 1827044f41c71883
---

Hey man,

Hope all is well!

Just wanted to check in and see if you had time for a call next week to
align on areas that might be good to focus on upcoming/where i can help,
finalize terms/agreement, and generally catch up.

Let me know when might work for you (or following week is fine too)!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-08-05
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: Loan repayment contract
msg_id: 1826ef1c1c9954ec
---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
Date: Tue, Apr 5, 2022 at 12:24 AM
Subject: Fwd: Loan repayment contract
To: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>


FYI - have a contract, but it’s in the UK and requires witnesses.

They’re willing to write it for Florida but would need someone else to look
at it for enforceability if yiu know someone who could - or can just do as
is with witnesses

Let me know what you think


---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Peter Bates <Peter.Bates@lewissilkin.com>
Date: Mon, 4 Apr 2022 at 07:26
Subject: RE: Loan repayment contract
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
Cc: David Willbe <David.Willbe@lewissilkin.com>


Hi Matthew



Sure - we could import the governing law clause from the Promissory Note to
make the waiver a Florida law document.  However, if we do that, I’m afraid
we would not be able to advise on whether the waiver letter would be
legally effective or enforceable.



Just so you’re aware, the reason the letter needs to be signed as a deed
under English law (and therefore needs to be witnessed) is because the
waiver is being given for no consideration (as the repayment of the loan
has already happened).



Very happy to make the change to Florida law if you want to proceed with
that, please let me know.



Kind regards



Peter



*Peter Bates* | Associate | *Lewis Silkin LLP* | T +44 20 7074 8151 | M +44
7503 547255



*From:* Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
*Sent:* 01 April 2022 23:59
*To:* Peter Bates <Peter.Bates@lewissilkin.com>
*Cc:* David Willbe <David.Willbe@lewissilkin.com>
*Subject:* Re: Loan repayment contract



*CAUTION:* External email.

Hi Peter,



Thanks for this. I'm guessing you wouldn't be able to draft it as a US
document (or does that even get around the need to have a witness)? If not,
this is fine as is.



Much appreciated!



Best,



Matthew

Hi Sam,

Sorry for the late reply, I've been in quite a hectic period of travel.

Please find bank statements attached.

Please find expenses linked here (although most will not have VAT):
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1u2n4eES-PL3GA9-Ifb3NBqozPXCPdyl8?usp=sharing

For reference, I have been keeping my receipts (including ones paid by my
personal cards that need to be reimbursed) here:
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1SU1X85Nye28YlCawN3RnemAecg5-MdIJ?usp=sharing

I've also updated my pending invoices to reflect which ones have been paid
in Xero.

Let me know if there's anything else you need from me.

Also, please do let me know when we'll be able to get 2020 annual
statements for Vang Advisory - I am unable to delay these filings further,
and I need to get them to my US accountant sometime in the next few weeks
so he can process them in time for the deadline.

Thanks,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-07-24
to: pelin sahin <pgsahin@gmail.com>
subject: airbnb
msg_id: 1822d92139282d82
---

Calle San Miguel, 25 bajo izda, Eguíllor, Navarra 31172, Spain

Hola! Bienvenid@s a nuestro hogar. Apezetxea es su nombre (la casa del cura) y
forma parte de un ‘Palacio gótico' o ‘Palacio de cabo de armería' (doble torre,
cuerpo central y patio de armas) catalogado como ‘patrimonio arquitectónico’
cuyos primeros vestigios datan de finales del s. XV.
Aquí os pasamos alguna información y sugerencias para que vuestra estancia sea
lo más cómoda y agradable.
Su habitante habitual Bárbara os proporcionará las llaves cuando lleguéis a
Egillor; mejor llamarle cuando estéis en el pueblo ya que ahí hay cobertura pero
no en el último tramo de carretera antes de llegar. Ella también os dará las
indicaciones de la casa.
La hora de llegada, normalmente es entre las 15,00 y las 23,00 y la de salida el
último día a las 14,00.
Primero entráis en un patio interior que compartimos con nuestr@s vecin@s Ander
y Susana. Su casa es la de la derecha y la nuestra la de la izquierda. Hay una
gata nuestra que habitualmente entra y sale de la casa pero simplemente cuando
lo intenten no las dejéis entrar, y si entran, sacadla. Nuestros vecinos se
ocupan de ella.
En la casa hay detector de incendios y un extintor por cada planta. El teléfono
de emergencias de cualquier tipo es el 112, como bien sabéis.
El wifi está permanentemente encendido y la contraseña es 8 ceros, es decir:
00000000. Tenéis todos los utensilios necesarios para las comidas. Por favor, no
meter las sartenes en el lavavajillas y limpiar solo con la esponja azul que no
raya. Las pastillas del lavavajillas están a la izquierda de la fregadera,
abriendo la puerta esquinera.
Para comprar comida los sitios más cercanos son Ororbia (saliendo del puente , a
la derecha unos 5 minutos. Comercio “A mano”) o Irurzun (saliendo del puente a
la izquierda unos 10 minutos).
La tv y el reproductor dvd tienen sus mandos al lado. El aire acondicionado está
en la segunda planta. Normalmente no os hará falta pero si hiciera mucho calor
dentro de casa, cerrando todas las puertas menos la de la escalera, refresca
toda la casa hasta abajo.
Si hiciera un día de mucho viento, mejor no dejar la sombrilla abierta.
La lavadora solemos  usar siempre en modo MIX a 30 grados. Para tender  lo
hacemos en el patio, hay 3 tendederos.
Por el jardín podéis acceder al huerto y recoger tomates (son muy buenos),
calabacines, alubias verdes, pimientos, etc. Para los restos orgánicos hay un
contenedor (compostaje) a la entrada del huerto. Por favor echad ahí todo lo
orgánico porque sino  olerá mucho en la basura de la cocina. En ella solo basura 
 inerte.
Tercer  finde de agosto son las fiestas del pueblo así que si os coinciden,
disfrutadlas.
Hay ventanas con mosquiteras como veréis, si las abrís y cerráis con cuidado
podéis refrescar la casa sin que entren moscas.
En la ducha, mejor usar solo la alcachofa pequeña ya que la grande está rota y
no sale el agua muy bien.
Si se va la luz, el reloj del horno se desconfigura y entonces éste no funciona.
Hay que configurarlo otra vez (poned cualquier hora) para que funcione de nuevo.
En las habitaciones podéis usar las partes de los armarios que están vacías. Las
camas tienen sábanas limpias puestas. Para los que duerman en la torre, hay
cortinas fijas en todas las ventanas menos en una, que tiene una cortina de
quita y pon con velcro. Importante porque son las ventanas del Este por donde
entra un sol potente a la mañana!
Hay un pequeño botiquín de curas en el armario del baño y otra caja metálica de
color azul con medicamentos en el vajillero de la cocina.
Y creemos que ya está! Para cualquier cosa que necesitáis  llamad a Bárbara
699801555.
Esperamos de verdad que disfrutéis de nuestra casa que ahora es la vuestra.
Bienvenid@s!

LimeWire Originals partners with Ushuaïa & HI Ibiza - You're invited to the

We’re thrilled to announce our first of its kind partnership with club

<https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xOTk5NTE2NDE0MzQzMzg4MTMxJmM9cTVmOCZlPTAmYj05OTY0MTk5ODAmZD1uMXUxZjRr.jGZZY0e1Tgr7BGb4pet2b4MRLV1epNnJi93h-BRjgK4>
.

*THE SHORT AND SWEET*

All LimeWire Original NFT holders — LimeWire’s first & exclusive NFT
collection — will enjoy two main benefits this season:

   - On Sunday, July 31st LimeWire Original owners get exclusive access to
   a LimeWire VIP Area + open bar in Ushuaïa.
   - LimeWire Original owners will gain access to both Ushuaïa & Hï Ibiza
   for the whole season of 2022 as part of a unique music experience-related
   utility.

*As part of our partnership, we are opening up a batch of 500 tickets to
the next mint of our LimeWire Originals collection. *

*You are invited* - Click below
<https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xOTk5NTE2NDE0MzQzMzg4MTMxJmM9cTVmOCZlPTAmYj05OTY0MTk5ODQmZD1yNWs3czZl.S-oKbOfTF6e9JZqbBTc-T9sZuIFCME9pfYFeLJFUKyk>
if you want to be part of this unique experience but don’t hold a LimeWire
Original yet. *Get one of 500 LimeWire Original NFTs* as part of our next
mint to gain access.
PURCHASE YOUR LIMEWIRE ORIGINAL NFT
<https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xOTk5NTE2NDE0MzQzMzg4MTMxJmM9cTVmOCZlPTAmYj05OTY0MTk5OTQmZD1uMGIyYTZn.j238Fsxc8S1qQoxfgSJciwLVw3eax8hlXnsTHVJiEOY>

*THE LONG BUT IMPORTANT*

Owning a LimeWire Original grants you access to the highest level of
membership LimeWire has to offer. Utilities range from *access to exclusive
LimeWire parties*, *music NFT drops*, a *$LMWR token airdrop*, *limited
merch* & more. LimeWire’s first party was hosted in New York a couple of
weeks ago, and the second, well you guessed it!

On Sunday, July 31st, LimeWire Original holders will get exclusive access
to a LimeWire VIP Area with an open bar in Ushuaïa. By purchasing one of
the LimeWire Originals — a limited collection of 10,000 Original NFTs —
collectors will automatically gain free entry into both legendary Ibizia
clubs, Ushuaïa & Hï Ibiza for the entirety of 2022.

*Tell Me More*

Voted by DJ Mag as one of the world's best clubs, LimeWire Original owners
will be able to experience Ibiza’s number one outdoor club — Ushuaïa — and
enjoy performances from New King of Reggaeton Ozuna and GRAMMY-nominated DJ
Snake who are set to headline the event. LimeWire Original owners will
experience a unique combination of Latin music & EDM stars in a perfect
open-air setting with the club's famously high tech and high spec
production.

*PS:* Do you want to own a LimeWire Original but not ready to purchase one
in the pre-sale? We're running a retweet-challenge
<https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xOTk5NTE2NDE0MzQzMzg4MTMxJmM9cTVmOCZlPTAmYj05OTY0MjAwMDAmZD1hNGk1ajVo.k6qE8NJW8bqq-O9VcyOO7zqYSRc_GFCJwUragbdwiVI>

to learn more.

date: 2022-07-21
to: brandon.ooi@samba.tv
subject: Fwd: Grin Gaming + Smile Coin Web3 Play&Earn
msg_id: 1822190fc27020c7
---

As mentioned on our call yesterday, my friends platform that facilitates
micropayments for his platform and some gaming companies (smile sdk
mentioned in the 3rd paragraph). Could be interesting if we wanted to go a
more crypto oriented route (which I don’t think we really are, but maybe it
could evolve that way), or to facilitate some value transfer within
platform before token launch

Grin is also maybe has some relevance - if we did something with prediction
markets (gamification/engagement is definitely an area we have mapped in
our potential product features), Nick could probably help. And he’s also
generally down to chat on various blockchain technical problems (he’s
CEO/CTO) - I think he’s also come up with an interesting solution for
custodied wallets for their users

Just let me know if you want to connect



---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Nick Bucheleres <nick@gringaming.com>
Date: Wed, Jul 20, 2022 at 4:36 AM
Subject: Fwd: Grin Gaming + Smile Coin Web3 Play&Earn
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>



*Nick Bucheleres*
CEO
630.247.7010
*gringaming.com <http://gringaminginc.com>*


Sent via Superhuman iOS <https://sprh.mn/?vip=nick@gringaming.com>


Our platform Grin Gaming <https://gringaming.com> is interesting in that we
are both *Web2* *and* *Web3 Play&Earn* real-money prediction gaming.  We
started the business 2 years ago when everything was Web2, but as we saw
the gaming landscape shift around us, we knew we had a major opportunity to
innovate in the Web3 Play&Earn space.

The way we solved this problem was by creating Smile Coin
<https://smilecoin.us> (built on Algorand).  Smile Coin is a utility token
and associated dApp <https://app.smilecoin.us> that rewards wallet holders
hourly based on their play volume.  We originally built a payment gateway
(ie the crypto PayPal) for Grin Gaming, so that we could accept Smile Coin
payments, and the hockey stick growth we saw in revenue, retention, and LTV
indicated to us that we were on to something huge.

We then created the Smile Coin payment gateway SDK
<https://docs.smilecoin.us/smile-coin-web3-sdk/integration/initialization>
that 10+ gaming companies are now integrating, so that they can become Web3
Play&Earn games with less than 10 lines of code, effectively over night.

TL;DR we have build a cutting-edge B2B2C blockchain protocol that allows
platforms to accept payments in crypto and rewards players for high play
volume,  thus driving massive growth in retention and LTV.

*Nick Bucheleres*
CEO
630.247.7010
*gringaming.com <http://gringaminginc.com>*


Sent via Superhuman <https://sprh.mn/?vip=nick@gringaming.com>

date: 2022-07-18
to: James Palmer <jcpgamerdating@gmail.com>, Qomoo Atomsa <qomoo@webblen.com>
subject: James/Qomoo intro
msg_id: 1821300d10b2b12e
---

Hi James,
Sorry for the cold email, but I figure  you'd be interested in connecting with
Qomoo - I've been advising his company Webblen for, which is a tokenized
community engagement platform built on Algorand, and is wrapping up a
funding round and looking to evaluate marketing paths in the future. He also
advises a very interesting modular/accessible hardware gaming controller startup
that also sounds like they might be interesting for both influencer marketing
and maybe from the perspective of GamerDating as well.
Qomoo, please meet James, who on the one hand runs a very successful influencer
marketing agency that did some very good work for SMILE coin, and has his hands
in and a deep network in the UK startup investment ecosystem. He also runs 
Gaamerdating, the largest dating site for video game enthusiasts.
i have  a feeling there are a few fronts you'll have interesting conversations
on - will  let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2022-07-16
to: Sam Cobbina <Sam@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>, Kevin Gardner <kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Following up on last email: VA wind-down, VA annual statement, and delayed filings/balances on accounts
msg_id: 182050a439270774
---

Sorry for the double emails but thought this might be helpful - please find
attached the end of year balance sheet i need for Vang Advisory (through Dec
2021 instead of 2020) - which is definitely the most urgent thing from the list
below. Hopefully this makes things a bit easier/clearer.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-07-16
to: Sam Cobbina <Sam@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>, Kevin Gardner <kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Following up on last email: VA wind-down, VA annual statement, and delayed filings/balances on accounts
msg_id: 18204c54b85d7cf4
---

Hi Sam and Kevin,
I haven't heard back from you on my  last email nearly a month ago, so I want to
make sure we are pushing things forward.

1) I still need an annual statement for Vang Advisory (not Capital, as we had
generated previously) for the year 2021 for my personal US tax filing. My
extended filing deadline is early September, so ideally I need this by END OF
AUGUST AT THE LATEST.
2) i believe that we have missed a few filings at this point for Vang Advisory,
and believe we have some upcoming, Please let me know how we can check on
this/get caught up
3) I also think I have overpaid my corporation tax payment plan due to poor
customer support from their phone line who told me my final direct debt had
failed instead of being processed (or maybe it was VAT tax) to HMRC - how can I
see this and receive a refund? (I know i am  also owed a refund for personal tax
as HMRC did not deposit my personal refund, but i have  not had a chance to call
them).
4) As I have been asking for the better part of this year, i would like to wind
down Vang Advisory - please let me know how we should push this forward, At this
point I am being charged more than £300 per month for accounting for what is
effectively an inactive company, so would prefer to cease this immediately. I
understand that maybe we need to make adjustments to other fees in light of vang
capital and personal tax filings (which hopefully we can look to wind those down
in the next 6 months or so as well). All to say, I am getting concerned by the
lack of clarity or progress in starting to wrap these items up, the cost
associated with not wrapping them up, and having to continue to worry about UK
filings and tax.

Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-07-16
to: Tom Winstanley <tom.winstanley@nttdata.com>
subject: Re: Tom W/Matthew Eisner catch up
msg_id: 18204aa7197b4011
---

Hey Tom,
Hope you're doing well and having fun at the Open!
i'll  likely be in Europe for the last week of July and first of August if you
want to try to plan for a call then?
Also, I did want to flag that getting my final payslips will soon be somewhat
urgent for me for some tax filings due at the end of August. It looks like I was
supposed to receive a final one via email in May according to my offboarding
email, but I never did receive it. Is there someone I can contact to get this?
Best,
Matthew

date: 2022-07-13
to: Qomoo Atomsa <qomoo@webblen.com>, Colin Allison <colin.allison92@gmail.com>
subject: Qomoo/Colin intro
msg_id: 181f60b69a8364b6
---

Hi Qomoo,
Please meet Colin, who I've been friends and worked with on projects over the
past 5 or 6 years. He's been leading the SE Asia ops for a number of years for a
management/innovation consultancy, and is in the process of setting up a web3
venture studio in Singapore that I'm helping with.
Colin, please meet Qomoo, who is a cofounder at Webblen, an Algorand-based
platform for community engagement that i've been advising for a few years and
will be raising soon. He also has his hands in some very interesting projects in
emerging markets, and is advising a few interesting startups (which i might look
to collaborate on a bit in the future).
I imagine you two will have a very interesting conversation :)
Best,
Matthew

Matthew

---
date: 2022-07-10
to: matthew.eisner@samba.tv
subject: token mechanisms notes
msg_id: 181ea30068dffc2f
---

Important questions:-What blockchains are we building on-Do we want to launch an
invite-only product-Do we want to do a press release when we launch our
validation pages-How will we generate protocol revenue

Samba consumer gtm    -Validation    -Landing pages    -Sign up form with data
capture and email verification    -PPC ad campaigns    -PR campaign announcing
intention to bring consumer product to market    -Ad pixels    -Ad creative   
-Ad campaigns    -Survey    -Batch email send tool    -Prelaunch    -Product
feature design/development    -Discord/telegrm  channel launch    -Prelaunch
website polished    -Brand and asset creation    -Product promo video    -AMAs 
  -Prep PR and secure exclusives    -Launch social and other branded channels   
-launch website    -Contests    -Pre-launch signup form/list position   
-Referral program    -customer data setup (acqusition, attribtution, engagement)
    -Email newsletters    -whitepaper    -pick blockchains, apply for grants   
-App Launch    -Public PR launch    -Product hunt launch    -Tutorials/FAQ   
-Basic support    -drip product onboarding/activation email/notification
sequence    -Post Launch    -Product demo video    -influencer marketing   
-partner marketing    -community calls    -ongoing recurring campaigns/limited
time reward incentives/partner based marketing    -ppc campaign launch and
scaling    -snapshot voting for ongoing feature development    -securing of
commercial PoC clients    -reengagement email/notification sequences    -launch
a/b testing    -expand website content    -swag-Token Launch-partnerships with
exchanges, market makers, liquidity providers, listing sites, lending/borrowing
protocols, on/off ramps, etc-OTC sales-Airdrops-engagement promos-trading
competitions-public sale-smart contract audits-foundation/grant/ecosystem
development



Reward mechanisms:-General principles-Token amounts from rewards should decrease
over time as token value goes up and distributed/circulating token supply
increases-We may want to look at a bonding curve to control price-How can we
create demand pressure for the tokens - what will their utility be, who will buy
them on the other side of the marketplace/protocol-Tokenomics-We should plan
distribution for at least 10 years-5 year vest, 1 year cliff for team-Team and
insiders should not exceed 10% of allocated token supply-We should allocate
between 30-50% of our token supply to treasury/foundation to allow for future
control of token price-Mechanisms-Tokens in exchange for demographic data-Tokens
in exchange for watch time (capped per day to discourage binging behavior)
-Tokens in exchange for or multipliers for streaks-tokens for creating an
account, pairing device-Additional tokens for watching specific/sponsored pieces
of content/ads-tokens for days device is active -Tokens in exchange for
processing power on device-tokens in exchange for linking streamling  platforms,
social networks, browser, etc-tokens in exchange for survey responses-tokens in
exchange for making recommendations peole  like-tokens in exchanges for ratings
-general TCR-airdrop to all active users (3-4 waves)-bonding curve token sales
-tokens in exchange for achieving levels-tokens in exchange for leaders/"top
performers'-staking rewards-tokens in exchange for onboarding friends/based on
how many tokens they ear-token reflection/tax/burn on trades-tokens in exchange
for LP providers-tokens in exchange for usage of a persons data/"protocol
revenue"-token bonus for first xxxx number of users

Ideas-urban mesh networks?


I miss searching for movies in blockbuster 
attribution of brand mentions in content to physical/digital sales

date: 2022-07-08
to: finance@shieldaccountancy.co.uk
subject: Final invoice and discord boost handover
msg_id: 181dc0e0b3988897
---

Hi Pawel and Anais,
Please find attached my last invoice for the month of June. We had discussed
billing 1/2 of the normal amount, but I've gone ahead and adjusted to 1/3 as
there were a few loose ends that I hadn't gotten around to tying up.
I also wanted to raise  that I'll be unboosting the discord server soon, so
someone will need to put that on another card if you want to maintain the custom
join link. I'll plan to do this towards the end of next week, and imagine the
payment will still be active for a few weeks after, so hopefully this gives you
time to add 12-13 boosts to the server (about $50/month)
Let me know if you have any questions or there's anything else, and hope all is
well!
Best,
Matthew

Hey Yohann,
Good to hear from you!
All's been good on my end - recently started a new role where I'm designing and
bringing their first consumer-facing (and maybe blockchain) product to market
for Samba TV, but am just getting through the onboard so starting to get a
little bit of time back.
More than happy to have a (or somewhat regular) chats with the marketing
assistant. I imagine having some execution legwork is definitely helpful, and I
saw the post on top 5 accelerators, which looked pretty solid at first glance :)
And congrats on the continued MRR growth!
And more than happy to do a call next week. How's either end of day Monday or
first thing Tuesday next week for you?
Best,
Matthew

date: 2022-07-08
to: Qomoo Atomsa <qomoo@webblen.com>, Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Qomoo/Ethan intro
msg_id: 181db5cc1573b3e5
---

Hi Qomoo,
Please meet Ethan, a successful serial entrepreneur in SF working on an audience
engagement product around data-driven creators/communities/comms. I imagine you
could provide him with some useful product strategy feedback as he looks at how
to bring Glystn to market.
Ethan, please meet Qomoo, who is one of the cofouders and leads commercials at
Webblen, who I've advised for a few years as they've built out a community
engagement/incentivization platform on the Algorand chain. They're also looking
to raise funds soon, and I thought you might have some tips for them around that
given you were looking to do the same in the near future.
I'll let you two take it from here :)
Best,
Matthew_____________________________

date: 2022-07-01
to: John Montag <johnmontag@montagwealth.com>, Ben Harris <benharris@montagwealth.com>
subject: Following up on call
msg_id: 181bc2ce517b3537
---

Hi Ben and John,
Thanks for your time the other week to discuss managing the trust.
I just wanted to follow up on a few points we discussed:
 * I'd like to allocate about 5% of the trust or $30k to buying bitcoin (let's
   say whichever is greater) via the Grayscale ETF. Ideally I'd like to DCA
   these transactions, say $2k on a monthly basis, or more ideally $500 on a
   weekly (or even $45 or so on a daily)

 * Please do take a slightly more aggressive strategy in general in terms of
   allocation, including money markets as I believe you mentioned

 * I'd like to withdraw $30k from the trust to a personal account


Let me know if there's anything else or you have any questions (or need account
information).
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-07-01
to: Alexander Potter <alex@coglabs.io>, Qomoo Atomsa <qomoo@webblen.com>
subject: Alex/Qomoo intro
msg_id: 181bbfaf915c4eb0
---

Hi Qomoo,
Please meet Alex, who has run a number of accelerator and startup programs with
very interesting partners, and works with a wide variety of startups to support
them in fundraising, strategy, and growth. He recently ran the Oasis/ROSE
accelerator programs, and i believe will be running one for Waves soon.
Alex, please meet Qomoo, who is the CRO for a web3 community startup building on
Algorand called Webblen. They're raising soon, which I thought you might have
some tips/connections for them potentially, but i thought more primarily it'd be
interesting for you connect with Qomoo as he's working on (and i'll likely help
a bit) building a consultancy/advisory around strategy/fundraising/biz
dev/partnerships/growth, and has his hands in some very interesting projects.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

starting my new gig.
How's your schedule the  next few weeks? Or any chance you're in SF week after

coming months?
Hope all is well!
Matthew

---
date: 2022-07-01
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Potential Intro/Thanks/Catchup
msg_id: 181bb9bb52cb6d79
---

Hi Ethan,
Hope all is well with you!
First, I wanted to thank you for the intro to Charley - it looks like I'll be
leading the consumer product and go to market for them for the foreseeable
future :)
Second, I wanted to see if you might want to connect with the founders
of https://www.webblen.io/ - I've advised them for a few years, and they've been
building a tokenized community engagement platform on Algorand, and will likely
raise some funding soon.
Third, I'll be in SF from the 13th to 16th - I'd love to take you to lunch or a 
coffee similar if you're around as a thanks for the intro to Charley and to
catch up in general. Let me know if that might work for you!
Best,
Matthew_____________________________

date: 2022-06-29
to: matthew.eisner@samba.tv
subject: (no subject)
msg_id: 181b0d24e215fe76
---

assets for validation:-3 segments (product buckets)-5 end-consumeer  targeted
USPs for each segment to test-5 image sets for each segment to test-1 landing
page with signup form for each segment-3 post sign up form thank you pages (
idelaly  with links to socials)-signup confirmation email 

Requirements:-ad placements (est $3k total budget)-5 x 5 images in various sizes
for each segment (same image can be used in multiple segments) - total of 75
images-copy for ads -copy for landing pages-deployment and hosting of landing
pages -tracking and conversion tracking of landing pages-utm urls-database for
signups-tool to deliver email

Funnel:-ads (ideally twitter, reddit, youtube) with broad audience targeting,
ages 18-30 or so, interest in crypto-traffic sent to 3 landing pages (1 for each
segment) where users can pre-sign up for access-Pause non converting creative,
optimize ad copy/imgaes/combos until clear winners for each segment (10k
impressions per ad, 30k ads, est $1500 assuming $50 CPM)-Pause campaign after
500 visitors to each landing page, look at over all  conversion rate, ad click
through rate, and submission per click rate for each after ($1500 assuming $1
CPC)-survey to signed up users asking which option they'd prefer to double
validate?

How to validate?

lists of engineers and designers
Engineers:-Nolan Astrien-George Muresan-Dylan Stone Miller-Gabe O'Leary-Dan
O'Leary-Remy Barooukh


-can we send infrared  sequence (a la remote) to open up samba  opt in? whats 
the fastest way to trigger samba opt in? or does it depend on hardware?
Passive crypto mining on TVs? Become Helium partner/node operators?
Video id reverse lookup from an image/frame/quoteSubtitle management

MISALIGNMENT: if we can't find a viable commercialization/buyer for the token
that consumers receive when they provide data/consume content/opt in, then
there's not really a strong use case for a token as opposed to a set of
consumer-facing products that provide bsic  utility to them

date: 2022-06-27
to: matthew.eisner@samba.tv
subject: notes
msg_id: 181a713022a47a63
---

Basic shared Features-consumner  facing; account creation, contact info/identity
capture, demographic/occupation/other data capture, email opt in, crypto wallet
connect?-internal/b2b facing: imporvement  of device/household graph, creation
of individual graph, validation of graphs and models, additional data
capture/enrichment with demographic/economic/occupational/other data-platforms:
iOS, Android, browser, native desktop?, web app?, smart TV app, apple TV app, TV
OS?, ipad app, wallet connect?
-Market/Generaal Research platform / surveys/focus groups/screen tests/etc
-Initial Consumer features: get paid or eclusive  access to content for survey
responses, content library/bookmarking-Other consumer features; recommendations,
rewards exchangeable/share acess  to data gives opportunities to watch eclusive
/get early access to content, manually validate/input data or train models,
import data from 3rd party platforms, data preference management-Potential
internal/commercial products: subject pools.survey research. enriched audiences
with opted in PPi, augment/enrich existing data products, improve
existing/create individual level graphs, augment existing data products with
demographic segments, open source data-long term vision: prerelease  screenings
for feedback
(https://variety.com/2022/digital/news/netflix-subscriber-prerelease-screenings-feedback-1235268439/),
general marketplace/platform to facilitate two way interaction between audience
and brands/advertisers/creative properties-Competitors/Similars: Amazon mTurk,
Forester, Hacera
-Ad/audience network/identity/device graph-Initial Consumer features:  data
preference management, interface to add/link devices, basic ability to be
compensated for ads/content viewed, discounts on targeted products, eclsive/free
content for targeted fans-Other consumer features: monetization/ad selling
preferences,  import data from 3rd party platforms, collect data to move from
household to individual-Potential internal/commercial products: validation of
existing/creation of individual level identity graphs, media buying marketplace
-long term vision: cross device/cross platform ad network/dsp/audience targeting
tool, 3 sided marketplace with placement sites, ad buyers, and audiences,
influencer network graph-Competitors/Similars: AdShares, Basic Attention Token,
cheqd, Lens Protovcol, Big Ad Tech, Storj, imdb freevee, hulu free
-Data platform/marketplace-Initial Consumer features: data preference
management/control, data monetization preferences, basic micropayment/rewards
for incremental data collection-Other consumer features:  import data from 3rd
party platforms, browser data ingestion, recommendations, import facebook/social
data, cookie manager-Potential internal/commercial products: more granular
customer data/segments/audiences, integrations with third party ad
networks/dsps/data marketplaces-long term vision: data marketplace, APIs/SKDs
for 3rd parties to plug our data in (likely paid for via tokenization or
protocol for converting fiat sales to token purchases)-Competitors/Similars:
Ocean Protocol, Chia
-operating system/ad/engagement/ecommerce/additive/permisioning  layer-Initial
Consumer features: recommendations, gamification (predictions, completion),
personal entertainment dashboard/portal-Other consumer features: data preference
management, content sequencing, smart TV app/project nightrider, social
viewing/content consumption, on TV/in content surveys, content launcher/routing,
parental controls-Potential internal/commercial products: more complete data
collection, validation of device graphs/household graphs/ACR, individual graphs
-long term vision: UI layer/OS for smart tvs and content consumption, object ID
and purchase/affiliate routing, social/collaborative content consumption
experience-Competitors/Similars: apple tv? chromecast? roku? prime video?
netflix? mad realities, life365
-rich content management tagging/indexing/licenscing
/referencing/matching/recommendations/bookmarking marketplace-Initial Consumer
features: content routing, content bookmarking, recommendation engine, logging
of content viewed-Other consumer features: data preference management,
audio/music recognition, social viewing/content consumption, manual user input
data capture, official 'moments' NFT marketplace, content launcher, parental
controls/smart content blocking using computer vision/just in time blocking for
parents/watch history digests-Potential internal/commercial products: micro
licensing marketplace, sponsored recommendations, mechanism to deliver
exclusive/early access to content-long term vision: consumer
content/entertainment tools, token curated registry-Competitors/Similars:
Opulous x Republic, Roku, Apple TV, Amazon Prime, NFt Royalties, Stoner Cats,
Mad realities, life365_____________________________

date: 2022-06-26
to: Christine Leuthold <ce3136@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Loan for credit card
msg_id: 1819ee8b61a5a66a
---

Zelle?

I’m buying more more quickly, although it admittedly is a bit risky. That
being said it feels undervalued to me right now. There’s always risk of
total crash, but I think it’s way less than 50% chance. and if there’s not
a total crash then it would be hard to imagine that Bitcoin and ethereum
don’t get back to or above their previous all time high.

I can lay it out in numbers but it gets confusing.

But I’m taking about 5% of my trust from carrit and putting it into crypto
over the next few months

---
date: 2022-06-26
to: Christine Leuthold <ce3136@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Loan for credit card
msg_id: 1819ec4056519f31
---

It’s actually slowing, but about 3k of this months was for medical expenses
for my shoulder

I’m increasing the speed in buying crypto (in dollar amount), to me I think
it’s 50/50 complete crash (actually probably less than 50 for this, but
just for arguments sake) or return to at least all time high prices (if not
higher), which logically that is a very attractive risk profile - not sure
you’re familiar with expected value equations? But I’m not looking to sell
these until end of 2023/2024 at the earliest, and even then i still
wouldn’t sell more than 25% of what I had then

---
date: 2022-06-26
to: Pat Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
subject: Re: Catch up
msg_id: 1819df81c25f1ab3

morning, or Thursday afternoon.

Let me know what might be ideal for you!

wrote:

again to cover my credit card bill? I need 6k if that’s doable.

is due, so I should be able to pay you almost everything back then, if not
middle of the month when my second payment comes in (as well as for phone
and rent)

Feel free to call before you go to bed tomorrow

subject: Re: Welcome to Samba! - Your First Day
msg_id: 1814f4a744fb3d8d
---

Hi Emily,

Thanks for your email, and looking forward to Monday!

I started the Bamboo onboarding, but came down with Covid on Tuesday of
this past week and have been a bit under the weather since. I’m feeling
pretty normal again though, so should be able to complete everything over
the weekend.

Sorry for any inconvenience!

Matthew

date: 2022-06-07
to: Greg Snoddy <greg.snoddy@samba.tv>

---

Hi Greg,
Great to hear :)
Yup, Emily Nguyen has been in touch with me about provisioning a laptop, and
I've gotten an onboarding sequence from BambooHR that I've started.
Let me know if there's anything else I should be expecting, and thanks for
your help!
Best,
Matthew

to: Kate Gray <kate@numberforty-one.com>
subject: Re: You are almost done with NTTD

---
date: 2022-06-07
to: Emily Nguyen <emily.nguyen@samba.tv>
subject: Re: Welcome to Samba! Mailing Address Request
msg_id: 1814084e117434cf
---

Hi Emily,
Thanks for your email, and excited to join the team!
I'd prefer a 13" Mac if possible - and my mailing address is:
2800 Neilson WayApartment 105Santa Monica, CA 90405

Let me know if there's anything else (apart from the Bamboo onboarding which i
got an email for :) )
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-06-07
to: Christine Leuthold <ce3136@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Home stuff while I’m gone
msg_id: 181406beecd29983
---

I tipped Patty an extra 20, and didn’t have her do your room because no one
had been in there. I’ll have her do it next time

I’ll send payment for all months when my first paycheck for my new job
comes, probably very start of next month

---
date: 2022-06-07
to: nassim@eternacapital.com
subject: Re: Invitation: Matthew <> Nassim catch-up @ Tue 2022-06-07 12:00 - 12:30 (PDT) (matteisn@gmail.com)
msg_id: 1813f7f734cac017
---

Hey man,

Super sorry for the late notice, and bit of a long story (I’ll give you the
full details when we talk but I hurt my shoulder pretty badly a few weeks
ago) - the short is Im okay, but have been trying to get Physiotherapy
appointments as part of my recovery, which has been very difficult to find
availability.

Anyways, I had the therapist call me just 15 minutes ago and said they had
a last minute cancellation in about an hour, which means I’d need to head
out pretty much when we’re slated to have our call.

Any chance we could move to the same time tomorrow or Friday (or I’m quite
flexible Friday in general)?

Let me know what works for you,

Matthew

few months for me. I hope all's well with you, and would love to catch up in the
coming weeks.
Also as a heads up, I'll be starting a new full time role at Samba TV leading
product for their crypto/web3 offering, so that should be fun :)
Hope to catch up soon!
Matthew

date: 2022-06-03
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Poplar
msg_id: 1812ba5a13acf50d
---

Looks interesting, but their min ticket is double the size of the fund. Happy to
chat with them on this though - would be curious how they're guaranteeing such
high yield based on defi, as assetsthere highly  volatile, rates are likely on a
long-term downward trajectory, and there is likely smart contract risk. I'm sure
a few of the blockchains I worked with might be interested in giving them a
grant though.
