---
date: 2022-05-27
to: Pawel Tomczuk <pawel@techtree.dev>
subject: Re: Notice
msg_id: 18102ebbe7770850
---

Thanks! I think it should be a pretty cool role :)

Sorry for the formality of this - and of course just to emphasize, zero
hard feelings from my side, just a bit of a too good to refuse scenario.
But please know zero hard feelings from my end, and would love to do
anything I can to make sure it’s as smooth a transition as possible for
TechTree.

Would be great to catch up in person as well soon! I’ll likely be in Europe
in mid to late July, and let me know if you have any plans for the US.

We can also discuss next week what handoff/replacement looks like, although
I’ve already started a bit of handoff discussion with Laurence today. I’m
traveling from Friday through Sunday, but will be on email Monday (Memorial
Day here) and working Tuesday

Have a good weekend!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-26
to: Greg Snoddy <greg.snoddy@samba.tv>
subject: Re: CV and scoping doc
msg_id: 180fe4a2913f79de
---

Hi Greg,

Sounds good!

I’m free tomorrow from 2-4 PM PST if that might work, or could maybe
squeeze something in late morning.

Friday through the long weekend I’ll be off-grid.

Quite flexible Tuesday from late morning as well.

Let me know whats ideal on y’all’s side.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-26
to: pawel@techtree.dev
subject: Notice
msg_id: 180fdce15c937da1
---

Hi Pawel,
I hope all is well and your trip in London  is going well.
I unfortunately need to give my 1 month notice effective today. We discussed a
few peripheral reasons as to why on our call earlier this week. But more
significantly, I just received an offer that is very difficult for me to turn
down, both in terms of the job/terms (senior PM for SambaTV, launching a web3
product that will effectively pay people to watch content and enrich/sell their
data to advertisers, with 50 million active users on their existing products, SF
based but remote, and 2x+ salary plus RSUs and tokens) and in light of the
current economic landscape (rents in LA spiked 20% in the last week alone - I
have cut pretty deep into my long-term savings for living expenses the past few
months, which isn't sustainable).
It's also hard for me to put so many eggs in one basket re: an investment and
working full time for such an early stage company, particularly given we've
effectively doubled our timelines for when we'd get a token to market and have
liquidity from what we had initially envisioned (which absolutely makes sense
strategically given realities of the market, but levers up my risk). All to say,
I need to take some steps to ensure I have a baseline comfort and spread risk
for the coming years.
I'd love to stay involved and help with TechTree however I can, whether just as
an investor/friend of the company, or more deeply. And I'd also certainly love
to talk about working together again when either the web3 offering or growth
needs of TechTree are a bit more developed and in the range of my core
competencies (integrating and configuring systems to scale customer
experiences/automate marketing/personalize user journeys, managing/ramping up
marketing operations, managing product/data-led growth, aggressively going to
market with a web3 product, optimizing marketing, quant user research,
onboarding/training team, etc), and there might be more budget available.
Certainly anything I can do to ease the transition though, please do let me
know.

Also more than happy to have a wider conversation around this, and either way
would love to try and maintain semi-regular catchups  on a few fronts. I'll also
look to see if I can't push us using TechTree for some of our web3 dev hiring at
Samba as well.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-25
to: Greg Snoddy <greg.snoddy@samba.tv>
subject: Re: CV and scoping doc
msg_id: 180fd838f4674005
---

Hi Greg,
Thanks very much.
I've reviewed everything in the offer letter, and all looks fine - I've signed
via docusign, but let me know if there's anything else you need from me.
Excited to be joining the team!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-25
to: Greg Snoddy <greg.snoddy@samba.tv>

msg_id: 180f89230d76e73c

Just a heads up I’m running about 5 minutes late. Talk soon!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-24
to: Greg Snoddy <greg.snoddy@samba.tv>
subject: Re: CV and scoping doc
msg_id: 180f7e8becb83dfd
---

5 or 5:30 both work well for me.

Looking forward to it,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-24
to: Greg Snoddy <greg.snoddy@samba.tv>
subject: Re: CV and scoping doc
msg_id: 180f744e0f34d588
---

Hi Greg,

Okay - do let me know.

In terms of conflicts, I don’t think any of my advisory roles would be in
any way competitive - happy to list these out if this would be ideal. And I
don’t think there should be any concern with IP or invention as long as
anything I’ve done previously is excluded.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-24
to: Greg Snoddy <greg.snoddy@samba.tv>
subject: Re: CV and scoping doc
msg_id: 180f723fecb4dd66
---

Hi Greg,

Thanks for the heads up, and me know how the approval goes.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-24
to: John Montag <johnmontag@montagwealth.com>
subject: Trust and financial planning
msg_id: 180f3ad2e196bfe2
---

Hi John,
Hope all has been well since we spoke some time back - our conversation around
metaverses seems to have been very prudent timing :)
My dad had mentioned I should reach out to you in terms of accessing the trust 
Carrit had left for me, as well as some topics of longer term financial/tax
planning as I bring my companies and investment assets on shore in the US from
the UK and plan for the future.
In terms of the trust, I was hoping to specifically withdraw $15-20k, and then
put another $50k or so in ideally some sort of interest bearing or tax
minimizing form of Bitcoin and Ethereum, as well as to discuss what might make
sense to do the remaining portion.
Let me know if it might make sense to hop on a call around this, or what next
steps might be.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-23
to: Greg Snoddy <greg.snoddy@samba.tv>
subject: Re: CV and scoping doc
msg_id: 180f1df6ab17345c
---

Hi Greg,

Great to hear, and looking forward to it :)

Let me know if there’s anything else you need from me.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2022-05-20
to: nick@gringaming.com, Gmuresan3@gmail.com
subject: Nick/George intro
msg_id: 180e246cba1e9412
---

Gonna keep this short as I've mentioned you both to each other and we all went
to UofM the same years -
George, Nick is the founder/CEO of Grin Gaming (prediction markets and other
games in web3) and SMILE Coin (crypto payments infrastructure/SDK).
Nick, George  has been working on GameStop's web3 products, and previously was
at Makersplace  and a number of other tech companies.
I think y'all might have an interesting conversation on a few fronts - will let
y'all take it from here!
Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-19
to: nick@gringaming.com, ross@zest.capital
subject: Ross/Nick intro
msg_id: 180de41903082564
---

Hi Ross,

Reconnecting (as I believe yall met at Decipher) you with Nick from
SMILE/Grin, and a longtime friend. He’s exploring some things around
Algorand NFTs that I thought Agash might be a useful contact for him with.
They’re also likely raising a round soon, and thought you might have some
ideas for them around that.

Nick, as requested :) reintroing you to Ross, who was in the Algorand
Accelerator program I ran. He’s super networked in the Algorand investor
ecosystem, and his cofounder Agash seems to have emerged as one of the
leading voices in Algorand NFTs.

I’ll let yall take it from here!

---
date: 2022-05-19
to: Kai <mukaiss@webblen.com>, cartersz@webblen.com, stanislas@techtree.dev
subject: Stas/Carter & Kai intro
msg_id: 180de3e176a551ad
---

Hi Stas,

Please meet Carter and Kai, who I met a few years ago in the early stages
of their company, Webblen. They received a sizeable grant from Algorand and
recently launched their ASA token, but are looking to go cross chain, and
were keen to connect with other L1s around grants.

Carter and Kai, please meet Stas, our CTO at TechTree and a serial
CTO/founder with a long history in the blockchain space. He’s connected
with a few grant programs and might be able to connect you with some.

I also imagine yall might be helpful in bouncing smart contract ey tech
questions off each other/etc.

I’ll let you all take it from here!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-10
to: chris@sambatv.com
subject: Fwd: Token contract clause and other follow up
msg_id: 180b00cc74c0f4d0
---

Hi Chris,

Just saw your note - l thought I had seen cjs@ as your email on the
calendar invite, but must have been mistaken. Please find the email with
mentioned language around the token below.

Let me know if there’s anything else, and looking forward to hearing next
steps (as well as my conversation with Brandon shortly).

Best,

Matthew



---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
Date: Mon, 9 May 2022 at 14:30
Subject: Token contract clause and other follow up
To: Ashwin Navin <ashwin@samba.tv>
Cc: <cjs@samba.tv>


Hey Ashwin and Chris,

It's been great chatting with you and learning more about how Samba TV is
thinking about bringing a token/web3 offering to market - definitely
excited about the idea of working on this with you :)

As I mentioned, please find pasted below some language that was presented
to me in a past contract and vetted by a UK lawyer with experience in
equity and token structures:

*Additional remuneration*

   - 1. Should the company issue a token, The Employee will receive the
   highest (or equally high) token allocation of all team members except of
   the founders of the company
   - 2. The Employee will receive an additional token grant that will be
   subject to the token sale or distribution results
   - The parties will agree on the details once tokenomics are finalized



Also, I think it might be good for me to have another conversation with
Brandon if it'd be possible to set that up. I just want to ensure we're
well aligned, as it sounds like I will be working significantly with him.


Additionally, I've had a few questions that have come to mind through our
conversations - I've pasted them below, but imagine some will need more
strategic discussion in the coming weeks, and others will likely be defined
by me in the early stages of my role.

   - Are there any strong visions for what the web3 product will look like?
   - Budgets, resources, and roadmaps
   - Timeline for launch(es) (sounds like ASAP, but as soon as 3 months?
   maybe more likely launch community & mvp product at 3 months, and token
   and blockchain product at 6 months?)
   - Are there stakeholders/investors/partners we need to manage as we move
   to launch a token/on web3? I imagine some strategic investors on the cap
   table have significant blockchain interests - at best would be good to
   try and leverage them for early partnerships
   - What are the current capabilities in terms of granularity of
   behavior tracking/content tagging? Specifically, can Samba recognize
   in-game progress/locations, or just the game that's being played? (thinking
   potential use cases around in-metaverse behavioural analytics)
   - What are current end B2C user touchpoints? Can we push on TV
   notifications or do we collect emails for users? What is current device
   opt-in rate and how has this trended?
   - How do B2B/enterprise clients currently interact with the product?



Please let me know if you have any questions, or if there's anything else I
can send along!

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-10
to: matt@techtree.dev
subject: TechTree to do
msg_id: 180af35d521ebfb6
---

-summary of data approach and user stories
-more detail on budgets/ideal outcomes
-review KPIs

---
date: 2022-05-10
to: Thomas Cremese <thomas@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Comms Plan % SEO works for Angels Partners
msg_id: 180acbbe2aa872e5
---

Hi Thomas,
Sorry for the late reply on this - I injured by shoulder  on an e-scooter and 
its  been a rough couple of weeks on my end.
1. I think the comms plan generally makes sense. I could see potentially  trying 
 to add a few more recurring posts later on in a few months once we have a bit
more solid templates and process together, but this seems like a good place to
start.
2. i don't have templates necessarily, but a few examples
- https://ideas.thefund.vc/p/flume-prashanth-vijay
- https://www.startupbootcamp.org/blog/2016/06/fintech-startup-week-policypal/ -
i think for the startup/angel of the month ones, its  more about having a
battery of questions we ask them - happy to help come up with this/add it to the
google sheet
3. no tools or templates in particular - i usually use a simple 3 identical
sections with image, text, and button in each to keep it as simple as possible.
mailchimp or sendgrid may also have good out of the box templates. but in
general, i think simpler is better, and even just 3 small images with a title,
sentence description, and link/button (and a basic footer with links/cta to come
to angelspartners) i think would be ideal
4. nothing in particular - i generally use buffer if thats  what you mean? for
image assets, i imagine one share image template with customizable text, a
couple of logos we can swap out, and a background image we can swap out for
various pieces of content, in various sizes for different platforms (or at least
 horizonal  and square). i also  generally use buffer  for scheduling if that's
what you meant?
5. i think  the beginnings of this make sense. reddit  and forums is a  bit
tricker, as there is definitly no exact process for this - it's all a matter of
trying to find the right community with a message. but i'd say it's about trying
to find relevant facebook or linkedin groups and posting there, reaching out to
niche vertical press about the posting, sending promo packages of pre-written
posts/copy/images to the startups/angels/scaleups/etc that are mentioned in
order to try and get them engaged and sharing the content so we can tap into
their audience. what  % of time is Sara engaged? this is stuff that's generally
a bit hard to allocate for on hourly basis, as its  a matter of trying a bunch
of things a few times and seeing if any seem to show signs of working that we
might want to focus on (as well as just generally trying to mention/highlight
partners in our content that have adjacent/larger audiences to us and engage
them to collaborate on/promote our content that features them.

should we plan for a call later this week or beginning of next potentially?
best,
Matthew

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-09
to: Ashwin Navin <ashwin@samba.tv>
subject: Token contract clause and other follow up
msg_id: 180aabb637ba17b3
---

Hey Ashwin and Chris,
It's been great chatting with you and learning more about how Samba TV is
thinking about bringing a token/web3 offering to market - definitely excited
about the idea of working on this with you :)
As I mentioned, please find pasted below some language that was presented to me
in a past contract and vetted by a UK lawyer with experience in equity and token
structures:
Additional remuneration * 1. Should the company issue a token, The Employee will
   receive the highest (or equally high) token allocation of all team members
   except of the founders of the company
 * 2. The Employee will receive an additional token grant
   that will be subject to the token sale or distribution results
 * The parties will agree on the details once tokenomics
   are finalized



Also, I think it might be good for me to have another conversation with Brandon
if it'd be possible to set that up. I just want to ensure we're well aligned, as
it sounds like I will be working significantly with him.

Additionally, I've had a few questions that have come to mind through our
conversations - I've pasted them below, but imagine some will need more
strategic discussion in the coming weeks, and others will likely be defined by
me in the early stages of my role. * Are there any strong visions for what the
   web3 product will look like?
 * Budgets, resources, and roadmaps
 * Timeline for launch(es) (sounds like ASAP,
   but as soon as 3 months? maybe more likely launch  community  & mvp product 
   at 3  months,and token  and blockchain product at 6  months?)
 * Are there stakeholders/investors/partners
   we need to manage as we move to launch a token/on web3? I imagine some
   strategic investors on the cap table have significant blockchain interests -
   at best would be good to try and leverage them for early partnerships
 * What are the current capabilities in terms
   of granularity of behavior tracking/content tagging? Specifically, can Samba
   recognize in-game progress/locations, or just the game that's being played?
   (thinking potential use cases around in-metaverse behavioural analytics)
 * What are current end B2C user touchpoints?
   Can we push on TV notifications or do we collect emails for users? What is
   current  device opt-in rate and how has this trended?
 * How do B2B/enterprise clients currently
   interact with the product?



Please let me know if you have any questions, or if there's anything else I can
send along!
Best,
Matthew

Matthew

brainstorms, and was keen to connect.
I'll let you two take it from here!

Matthew

---
date: 2022-05-05
to: Charley Lanusse <charley@samba.tv>, ashwin@samba.tv, brandon.ooi@samba.tv
subject: Following up from our conversations
msg_id: 18092c4ae2e38b93
---

Hi all,
First, I wanted to say I've very much enjoyed our conversations over the past
weeks, and think there are some very interesting ways Samba TV could approach
web3/blockchain/etc.
To follow up, I wanted to send along notes I put together of some rough (and
flexible) high-level phases of a "plan" for bringing a token and web3 product to
market, as well as a number of projects/protocols I think are similar or
complementary to what we might look to build:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1sAuZeLcVUG8hapmkHfHuNIs-ond1oruh0YOBIbCXQhA/edit?usp=sharing
This go to market "plan" is very much based on the assumption of a token and
web3/"decentralized" product launching simultaneously, which I know may not be
the case in reality, and would thus alter some of the timings of activities.
However, I thought it would be useful to start to map out some of the scope of
what this might look like for us to talk through.
Looking forward to meeting you on Friday, Ashwin, and anyone else please let me
know if you have any questions (or feel free to share with others that might
find it relevant).
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-04-26
to: James Lethem <j.d.lethem@gmail.com>, Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: James/Ethan Intro
msg_id: 18067699bdf4d244
---

Hi James,
Please meet Ethan, who has a very interesting background in bay area
startups/tech, and is  currently building a web3 platform for community comms
aggregation and data/automation that i think you'll find interesting, He's also
was looking some help in terms of some modeling work around platform flows and
revenue streams that I'll likely be helping with a bit that I think might be up
your alley.
Ethan, please meet James, a friend from London who has worked doing analytics,
modelling, and growth work in startups and for interesting ecosystem orgs, and 
has a quite  a bit of familiarity with and connections in web3 (and keen to get
more in the space in terms of his work).
Hope you two can find a time  to connect!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-04-26
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Re: time to catch up this week?
msg_id: 18066d7756bf19a6
---

Just told him this and sent him your LinkedIn -

“Wanna connect with a founder who is working on social analytics/automation
for discord/creators/communities?”


Frankly, this is a wider comment on him - he’s a decent friend of mine and
has been trying to build a similar product for 8+ years, and he’s decent on
product and not so great on tech or business. I think he has a fair amount
of the pieces from past similar projects, but the architecture is probably
a mess, and none of the data features you’re looking at. But I don’t think
he’ll frankly get far unless he partners up with some people

---
date: 2022-04-26
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Re: time to catch up this week?
msg_id: 18066c40d7cb7f0d
---

hey -
so re-pinged ben but haven't heard back from him
will ping socialstack founder today
breadcrumb angel only invests in european startups apparently, will see if he's
still open for a chat
dopewars guy is founder of this, https://rebel.fun/, has a view to build some
discord automation tools in the future and doesn't want to connect bc sees it as
competitive - was disappointed fixed pie mentality
also pinged my friend who does modeling work - he's currently quite heads down
prepping to set up a web3 venture studio in Singapore, but i have a feeling will
be up to connect
will also think on a few others

call (or meet in person if you're in LA in the coming weeks).
Best,
Matthew

date: 2022-04-21
to: Idan Beck <idanbeck@gmail.com>
subject: Following up from lunch
msg_id: 1804dc49ae623f6c
---

Hey Idan,
Was great to catch up last week, and thanks for lunch :)
i did  just want  to mention that i would  definitely be interested in working
for you and Akash at Epoch if it makes sense. I also realize  when I spoke to
Akash a few months ago, my timelines for stabilizing things at TechTree and
looking for my next role were probably a bit farther off. I am starting to talk
to some people about what's next for me a bit more actively now (although still
a few months out probably until I would ideally make a move), and certainly
would be excited to explore how we could work together again. And also more than
happy to reach out to Akash and chat with him again (I was hoping to drop him a
line next time I was in NYC, but still trying to get a visit in there  for the
first time in a few years).

Also a few potential intros i mentioned - let me know if any would be
particularly interesting: * Pat Sheehan  - my roommate in SF's dad, based in North Carolina, has a
   sizeable stake in Epic Games via his financial advisory/investment firm

 * Gabe O'Leary  - childhood friend from Atlanta, Engineering/CS at Michigan, 5+
   years at Microsoft, been working at a 5 or so person social bookmarking
   startup but looking for something new

 * Nick/Doug  - I mentioned Nick, a friend from college who has become a good
   friend in LA and is building SMILE Coin (payments rails SDK) and Grin Gaming
   (prediction markets) on Algorand, which I had done some work for. Very
   talented technically, CEO/CTO crossover, and would be very keen to chat with
   you about some systems architecture, as well as probably your take on leading
   both technical and business sides of companies simultaneously. Doug is his
   COO based in Burlingame with a very interesting background.

 * Yao Tang  - london based, ex-Improbable and Tencent, currently at TikTok
   gaming investment arm, ex software engineer in finance/banking, think she
   would really like what you're working on (and her husband is at Project A
   Ventures, which I believe has a strong gaming focus)


I had mentioned the CTO at Techtree, Stas, but he's a bit swamped right now. I
also have a few VC's and blockchain foundations that are giving out grants that
might be interesting, but sounds like that might not be a huge focus/problem
right now. If there is an aim to tokenize/web3 Epoch though, it might be good to
explore grants relatively soon, as they're equity free, and starting to become
more competitive/less available.
Also, I've included a little blurb below on where we are with TechTree if
there's anyone you think would be well aligned for us to connect with. Happy to
get specifics on amounts raised previously as well if that would be helpful, but
I believe it's < $2M @ < $10M valuation.
Let me know if there's anything else, or anything I can help with.
Best,
Matthew



At TechTree we arebuilding a platform where techies get rewarded for the help
they offer through their network, and knowledge.Here isa short deckoutlining our
vision, and our initial product to reach this is a SaaS solution for hiring
bounties. Here is a 96 secs video explaining how hiring bounties work. Since the
launch of this in Jan 2022, and we have had some amazing traction:

·15 new companies buying the SaaS subscription for bounties in 8 weeks since our
launch + 80 companies in the active pipeline

·7 confirmed VC pilot + 20+ in the active discussions about such partnerships

ojust the partnership with Tech Nation resulted in the introductions to 31
companies from their latest cohort of the Upscale program

·15 pilots with creators, influencers, and communities and over 200 of them on a
waiting list




We are about to close a strategic/angel round of $2M under a $30M capped SAFE,
with an aim to raise more funding from institutional investors later this year.
We have more than 80% committed, and we are looking for the last checks from
high value added people.

---
date: 2022-04-21
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Shifting call tomorrow
msg_id: 1804a1ac255c02c3

Hey Yohann,

Hope all is well. Im a bit exhausted getting back from SF today. Is there a
chance we can move our call to tomorrow or next week?

Best,

Matthew

date: 2022-04-20
to: Ben Perez <ben.perez@aragon.org>
subject: Potential intro and my new role
msg_id: 1804838edeab96b3
---

Hey Ben,
Hope all is well with you since we spoke a while back.
Very random, but a founder i work  a bit with (2x significant exits in SF, runs
a fund, building  a web3 social startup about to raise a very large round) and I
were messaging back and forth, and he sent me your article on DAO Tooling you
wrote recently. I mentioned I had talked to you a while back, and he was super
keen to connect if you might be interested
- https://www.linkedin.com/in/ethanfassett/
Also, in some personal news, I joinedTechTree as head of product to spin out our
web3 offering and token. We're already working with a bunch of major funds,
ecosystems, and blockchains to help them hire through their communities and
share incentives/rewards from doing so (over $600k in bounties paid out so far
to our users). If you might be interested in learning a bit more/seeing how we
could help enable your community to bring in talent, I'd love to set you up with
our VP of Partnerships.
Let me know if either of those might be of interest!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-04-20
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Re: Financial models for token monetization?
msg_id: 180481ad67832c18
---

Hey Ethan,
Sorry for the delay here - hectic few days of meetings and getting back to LA.
So certainly something I do a fair bit of (was the core focus of my role at NTT
DATA actually). I am vaguely starting to look for something new and longer term
in the coming months (as being a pre-seed angel and working for the same company
re: TechTree is a lot of consolidated risk for me), although I am a bit limited
in capacity for the coming months. But happy to chat.
i also  have a good friend who is very good at modeling work, organized/prompt,
and I think is open to some side projects at the moment
- https://www.linkedin.com/in/colinallison1 - I'm sure he'd be up to connect.
I do have one other friend who is quite good at modeling, but he's a little
flakey as of late, so hesitant to connect you. He is actually closer to the team
at Aragon (although I just dropped Ben an email, as I imagine my Discord message
was one of many for him with his role) than I am, and is the Venture Partner on
a fund in UK who's  founder is very media oriented that could be a good fit
- https://www.linkedin.com/in/jameslethem/
Let me know how those sound!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2022-04-19
to: sameersingh17@gmail.com
subject: Potential intro to founder
msg_id: 18043bf31e845394
---

Hey Sameer,
Hope all is well with you!
I was chatting with a founder in SF the other day I've known for a few years who
is about to raise for a product I thought you might find interesting - basically
aggregating audience/comms/social channels for creators with some interesting
tracking/analytics/insights from the data, and a view towards automation and
potentially some cool network effects.
Let me know if you might want to connect.
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ethanfassett
https://glystn.com/

Best,
Matthew

tip on these/general rule for email - most email spam filters scan the ratio of
images-to-text in an email as an indicator of spam - try and make sure less than
50% of the email vertically is images (ideally less than 1/3)

date: 2022-04-19
to: Jonathan Saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>, stanislas@techtree.dev
subject: Jonathan/Stas intro
msg_id: 180434928fc2029a
---

Hi Jon,
Please meet Stas, the CTO of TechTree (where I've been full time the past 6
months or so), who has a very interesting background in blockchain. We're
looking to bring on some more design resource  in the near future, and I wanted
to connect you two to see if there might be a good fit.
Stas, please meet Jonathan, who I've collaborated with now for about 6 years
including on projects for Startupbootcamp, aorist (the Algorand NFT platform),
and my personal and company brands, and has always done stellar work. He's also
been the designer for Radix for a number of years.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Matthew

---
date: 2022-04-15
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Re: Glystn Pitch RAW Draft - CONFIDENTIAL
msg_id: 1802e632812e43b4
---

Hey Ethan,
Some thoughts and comments for reference/us to chat through:
Web3 investor appeal - is there some decent web3 experience on team? Team slide
could be an issue (maybe push to the back in a web3 version) - but web3 also
often expects anon teams, so if you emphasize product, get web3ers excited about
that, and then mention an actual human team at the end, that could work well
Problem/solution good - evolving world of creators slide, shows a reasonable
web3 creator size, but doesn't show growth over past 2 years (as that's
basically been its entire timeline), or mention uniqueness of platforms used. 
although  going this route might pidgeon hole  you to web3, as opposed to 
present  2 attractive segments in web2 and web3. also  for successful creators
use  best-practice engagement statement, would  be great to have some sort of
insight or metric here. Also maybe mentioning audience ownership problem here
could be good (i.e. owned channels vs branded channels); showing web2/3
channel/platform overlap could be good
One foot in/out for web3 - i do think you need some context as to why token (if
you're tokenizing) or why blockchain. Will there be protocol revenue? Web3
integrations? etc?
SKALE integration: don't think ites  necessary to go into detail, just mention
it and maybe have an appendix slide you can send or just talk through it. web3
investors are usually pretty tech agnostic from what i've seen, although saying
too much about your tech or seeming to committed to a blockchain they dont like
can raise redflags
Biz model: happy to chat through
competitive landscape - lots of SME/individual tools taking off, more valuable
(but difficult to use) data for optimization, most SME/individual CRM plays try
to be too similar to previous CRMs and aren't focused on channels these users
actually communicate through and are too clunky and time consuming to set up
(and costly to integrate)
Roadmap: the nice part about web3 is you can be vague here, but likely will need
a whitepaper soon to start laying this out, and definitely some buzzwords that
you'll want to cover (TGE, CEX listings, testnet launch, mainnet launch, 
multichainn  launch, etc)

date: 2022-04-15
to: jonathan saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Capacity for work

---

Hey man,
All good on my end - spending most of my days in California at this point, but
likely will be in Europe a bit more in summer
Glad aorist is still coming along - that could end up being a very good thing to
have aligned with in the art world.
I'm basically working full time on this at the moment, and we likely need to
standardize and ramp up some of our design (although we're pretty happy with
branding and basics). https://techtree.dev/ - just wanted to feel out first
before I mention to our team, but likely will want to loop you with our CTO in
the coming week or two if you're up for it?
Best,
Matthew

date: 2022-04-15
to: "Gabe O'Leary" <oleary.gabe@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Techtree fundraise info
msg_id: 1802b2c4e7b9a771

No worries - any interest in connecting with the founder  of the first company I
worked at in SF? Now working on this and hiring devs as fast as he can
https://medium.com/epoch-ml/introducing-epoch-75f1bcad2add working on that,
raised some very sexy funding, brilliant guy, ex-MS. Meeting him tomorrow.
Also maybe worth connecting with Nick Bucheleres. I've gotten pretty tight with
him in LA, knew about what he was building before we reconnected (had done some
work on Algorand, and they're one of the biggest protocols on it), and think
there might be something interesting there.

Will def let you know re: PNW - thinking maybe late summer and try and swing to
Shambhala or Bass Coast maybe for my first festival in like 7 years.
Hope all is well man!

relevant statements for my dad's filing related to Vang Capital.
I will also be sending my wider info along in a month or so, but am still
waiting on more documents from my UK accountants (so will need an extension).
I have also recently set up US banking for Vang Advisory, which I would like to
get operational/transition away from using my UK company in the next month or so
ideally. I'll send a separate thread on this and a few other points next week.
Let me know if there's anything else.
Best,
Matthew

protocols.

date: 2022-04-05
to: josh@pascalcap.com
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17ffbc8b87edf95f
---

Hi Josh,
Was great to chat earlier. As mentioned certainly would  be interested in
hearing more the  deal you mentioned you're working on, and any others in the
future.
Also, please find below a bit of info about TechTree and our raise. If you might
be interested in using it for yourself/portfolio, or potentially putting some
money in, let me know and I can get the ball rolling on either with an intro
(although as mentioned the investment side is moving quickly and getting a bit
squeezed as we close out our round).
Let me know if there's anything else!
Best,
Matthew



At  Techtree  we are building a platform where techies get rewarded for the help
they offer through their network, knowledge and data. Here is  a short deck 
outlining our vision, and our initial product to reach this is a SaaS solution
for hiring bounties. Here is a  96 secs video  explaining how hiring bounties
work. Since the launch of this in Jan 2022, and we have had some amazing
traction:

 * 17 new companies buying the SaaS subscription for bounties in 8 weeks since
   our launch + 80 companies in the active pipeline
   
   
 * 7 confirmed VC pilot + 20+ in the active discussions about such partnerships
   
   
 * just the partnership with Tech Nation resulted in the introductions to 31
   companies from their latest cohort of the Upscale program
   
   
 * 15 pilots with creators, influencers, and communities and over 200 of them on
   a waiting list
   
   






In 2021 our alpha version had 500k USD in revenue (with approx. 1/3rd coming
from subscriptions) and included working with clients such as Advent, Ramp
Network or Wayflyer, funds such as Sequoia, Northzone, or EQT Ventures who all
continued working with us after the beta release.

In 2022 we’re planning to move our platform to web3. Infrastructure - we already
got grants from Algorand and NEAR. This will make our platform fully trustless
and transparent.

We’ve built a very experienced team of previously VC-backed founders, ex-VC, and
several engineers with 15+ years of experience, who have founded tech startups
before. We are backed by Frontline Ventures, Notion Capital, and fantastic
angels including the ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday, and senior execs from
Stackoverflow, Uber, and Monzo.

Currently, we are looking to raise a 1.5-2m USD round from top operators, which
will help us to get to 100+ subscription clients at the end of the year. We are
essentially oversubscribed, so please let us know as quickly as possible if you
would like to participate to ensure the best chance of being able to.

Wednesday or Thursday.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2022-04-04
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: Loan repayment contract
msg_id: 17ff6ade0addb40a
---

FYI - have a contract, but it’s in the UK and requires witnesses.

They’re willing to write it for Florida but would need someone else to look
at it for enforceability if yiu know someone who could - or can just do as
is with witnesses

Let me know what you think


---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Peter Bates <Peter.Bates@lewissilkin.com>
Date: Mon, 4 Apr 2022 at 07:26
Subject: RE: Loan repayment contract
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
Cc: David Willbe <David.Willbe@lewissilkin.com>


Hi Matthew



Sure - we could import the governing law clause from the Promissory Note to
make the waiver a Florida law document.  However, if we do that, I’m afraid
we would not be able to advise on whether the waiver letter would be
legally effective or enforceable.



Just so you’re aware, the reason the letter needs to be signed as a deed
under English law (and therefore needs to be witnessed) is because the
waiver is being given for no consideration (as the repayment of the loan
has already happened).



Very happy to make the change to Florida law if you want to proceed with
that, please let me know.



Kind regards



Peter



*Peter Bates* | Associate | *Lewis Silkin LLP* | T +44 20 7074 8151 | M +44
7503 547255



*From:* Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
*Sent:* 01 April 2022 23:59
*To:* Peter Bates <Peter.Bates@lewissilkin.com>
*Cc:* David Willbe <David.Willbe@lewissilkin.com>
*Subject:* Re: Loan repayment contract



*CAUTION:* External email.

Hi Peter,



Thanks for this. I'm guessing you wouldn't be able to draft it as a US
document (or does that even get around the need to have a witness)? If not,
this is fine as is.



Much appreciated!



Best,



Matthew

date: 2022-03-30
to: christof.jaritz@gmail.com
subject: Following up from LinkedIn
msg_id: 17fdc5567b8e1185
---

Hi Christof,
Hope all is well - just following up from our LinkedIn thread, please find below
a bit of a blurb about TechTree and a deck we've been sending around.
I'm actually based on West  Coast US these days, but it would be great to do a
call to catch up maybe Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday morning your time next week if
that works for you - or feel free to suggest some alternatives.
Looking forward to it!
Matthew




AtTechTree, we are building a platform where technical people get rewarded for
the help they offer through their network, knowledge and data. Here isa short
deck outlining our vision, and our initial product to reach this is a SaaS
solution for hiring bounties. Here isa 96 secs video explaining how hiring
bounties work. Since the launch of this in Jan 2022, and we have had some
amazing traction:
 * 13 new companies buying the SaaS subscription for bounties in 8 weeks since
   our launch + 80 companies in the active pipeline

 * 7 confirmed VC pilot + 20+ in the active partnership discussions
 * just the partnership with Tech Nation led to 31 intros to companies from
   their latest program

 * 9 pilots with creators, influencers, and communities and over 200 of them on
   a waiting list

 * $150,000 of revenue in March alone so far


In 2021, our alpha version had 500k USD in revenue (with approx. 1/3rd coming
from subscriptions) and included working with clients such as Advent, Ramp
Network or Wayflyer, funds such as Sequoia, Northzone, or EQT Ventures who all
continued working with us after the beta release.

In 2022, we’re planning to move our platform to web3 infrastructure - we already
got a grant for it from Algorand - one of top 15 blockchains in 2021.  This will
make our platform fully trustless and transparent.

We’ve built a very experienced team of previously VC-backed founders, ex-VC, and
several engineers with 15+ years of experience, who have founded tech startups
before. We are backed by Frontline Ventures, Notion Capital, and angels
including the ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday, and senior execs from
Stackoverflow, Uber, and Monzo.

Currently, we are looking to raise a 1-2m USD round from top operators, which
will help us to get to 100+ subscription clients at the end of the year.

---
date: 2022-03-30
to: Sheel Tyle <sheeltyle@gmail.com>
subject: TechTree socioeconomic layer for devs
msg_id: 17fd8ce0d33e3fd6
---

Hi Sheel,
Hope all is well since we last spoke a few years ago.
I'm reaching out as I was going through my LinkedIn trying to refresh my memory
on some of my network around afundraise I'm doing forTechTree, and remembered
you had invested in a few companies in similar spaces previously.
I've included a little snippet below and a deck linked we've been sending around
- but in essence, we enable companies and communities to monetize their networks
and share fees (typically paid to recruiters) for filling tech roles in
companies. We've already paid out $300k+ in bounties to our users for successful
hires made via referrals, and are raising from value-add angels/operators before
raising a larger institutional round later this year (and launching a web3
offering and token late this/early next year).
if you might be interested, have thoughts/feedback, or have any questions, I'd
love to find a time for a call (and just to catch up more generally). Or if you
might know anyone who might be interested in what we're working on, feel free to
forward our info to them/connect them with me.
We're getting in the final stages of our round, but want to try and bring on as
many well-networked and knowledgeable angels as possible.
Best!
Matthew

Hi Peter,
Hope all is well with you since we talked towards the end of last summer!
I wanted to reach out as I recently jumped full-time onto one of my portfolio
companies, TechTree leading our web3 product and token go-to-market, and thought
it might be of interest to you given your background.
We're in a fundraising mode (although I imagine wouldn't meet the criteria of
Bridge having no real connection to Florida), so certainly if that was of
interest, we're essentially waiving our minimum tickets to get interesting
people on board as we close out the round. But more generally, the product is
focused initially around network-based hiring for technical teams, and long-term
aiming to be a general economic layer for technical knowledge and networks. We
also have a long-term view of helping creators of technical content monetize
their audiences/value, which I thought might be particularly interesting to you.
I've pasted below a little bit of a blurb, and would love to find a time to
catch up to hear your feedback in general. I'm also hoping to make it to Florida
before it gets too hot, so will certainly let you know if i do  so we can
hopefully meet in person.
Best,
Matthew

Hey man, as mentioned please find below some info we're sending out around the
TechTree fundraise - any thoughts or feedback greatly appreciated!


At TechTree, we are building a platform where technical people get rewarded for
the help they offer through their network, knowledge and data. Here is a short
deck  outlining our vision, and our initial product to reach this is a SaaS
solution for hiring bounties. Here is a 96 secs video  explaining how hiring
bounties work. Since the launch of this in Jan 2022, and we have had some
amazing traction:
 * 13 new companies buying the SaaS subscription for bounties in 8 weeks since
   our launch + 80 companies in the active pipeline

 * 7 confirmed VC pilot + 20+ in the active discussions about such partnerships
 * just the partnership with Tech Nation resulted in the introductions to 31
   companies from their latest cohort of the Upscale program

 * 9 pilots with creators, influencers, and communities and over 200 of them on
   a waiting list

 * $150,000 of revenue in March alone so far


In 2021, our alpha version had 500k USD in revenue (with approx. 1/3rd coming
from subscriptions) and included working with clients such as Advent, Ramp
Network or Wayflyer, funds such as Sequoia, Northzone, or EQT Ventures who all
continued working with us after the beta release.

In 2022, we’re planning to move our platform to web3 infrastructure - we already
got a grant for it from Algorand - one of top 15 blockchains in 2021.  This will
make our platform fully trustless and transparent.

We’ve built a very experienced team of previously VC-backed founders, ex-VC, and
several engineers with 15+ years of experience, who have founded tech startups
before. We are backed by Frontline Ventures, Notion Capital, and angels
including the ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday, and senior execs from
Stackoverflow, Uber, and Monzo.

Currently, we are looking to raise a 1-2m USD round from top operators, which
will help us to get to 100+ subscription clients at the end of the year.

---

or two.
I'm working on bringing TechTree's token and web3 product to market, and would
love to generally pick your brain on a few areas around that (we have a few
grants but curious if there are others we should look at - and maybe some I can
set you up with; we also have a token + social token model we're considering,
and also happy to help on anything you're focused on if i can).

calendly I should book via.
Best,
Matthew

verify? Maybe just  me being  paranoid :)

Hope all's well with you - would love to hear what you're up to when you have
some time!

---
date: 2022-03-21
to: Josh Lee <josh@pascalcap.com>
subject: Re: Notification: Matthew/Josh Call @ Wed 2022-03-09 10:30 - 11:20 (CET) (Matthew Eisner)
msg_id: 17fae3c6d417a02c
---

Hi Josh,
Sorry for the delay on this - I had a very hectic few weeks doing a bit of a
roadshow and a company retreat in Europe.
I'm back in LA now - how's sometime Wednesday, Thursday, or Friday afternoon
California time? Or feel free to suggest some alternative options.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-21
to: Abhi Gupta <abhi@thirdbasecap.com>
subject: Re: Investments
msg_id: 17fad6115aeb4a6a
---

Hi Abhi,
Sounds interesting, but extremely  vague. I was an early investor in
deventraland, sandbox, somnium space, and cryptovoxels, built major corporate
metaverse infrastructure at NTT DATA, and have been tracking metaverses for 8+
years now. Please tell me what company this is before I agree to commit my time
on a call (as it's likely I already have a contact there).
i also am currently raisng  a round for http://techtree.dev/ - we help funds
like Sequoia, Notion, and Tech Nation support their portfolio startups attract
technical talent through referral programs (as well as scale ups and technical
content creators). We are spinning out a web3 product (with large grants from
Algorand, NEAR, and others) that will create additional monetization
opportunities for engineers that typically have been stolen from them by
recruiters, corporation, etc. We had mid 6 figure ARR last year and have paid
out over $600k in bounties for talent referrals to engineers on our platform.
We're closing our round in the next week or two, but let me know if you'd be
interested in learning more.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-21
to: Ahsan Sohail <ahsansohail22@gmail.com>
subject: TechTree fundraise info
msg_id: 17fad02f721132e8
---

Hey Ahsan,
Following up on our Whatsapp messages, please find below a blurb we're sending
around. We're likely looking to close the angel/operator part of our round in
the coming few weeks, but would be great to have you on!
Let me know if you have any questions, and I've cc'ed  Pawel as well if you want
to find the time for a call or have longer term strategic messages.
Best,
Matthew




TechTree is raising approx. 1-2m USD from top operators, influential groups or
micro-funds in web3. The operator funding round will enable us to strengthen our
team to keep up with existing demand, as well as speed up the execution of our
product roadmap. We'd be looking to get to 100 paying subscription clients in
the coming months and raise a VC round then.Here'sour deck and here'sa draft of
our white paper outlining our long-term vision of becoming a fully trustless and
transparent social network for tech people, where they can monetize their
personal assets. Since we've launched our beta version in Jan, we've got an
amazingly positive response from all our users - developers, clients, creators &
influencers, VCs and protocols. Here is a summary of our traction updates from
the start of Jan'22:1. Beta launch: We launched our beta in January and the
response from clients has been terrific - 13 new clients in the last 8 weeks.
Previously with our alpha version of our platform in 2021, we paid 300k+ USD in
bounties, had 500k USD in revenue, and partnered with VCs such as Sequoia, EQT
Ventures, and Northzone.
2. Go-to-market ramp up: Additionally, our go-to-market strategy focused on VC
partnerships is working very well, and funds have been super receptive to our
beta version. We’re launching our first 4 VC partnerships in the next month,
have a rapidly growing pipeline, and are in advanced talks with many new
partners. Notion Capital has featured us intheir March newsletter and Technation
introduced us to 31 Series A companies on Friday :)
3. Customizable Bounty Boards: In mid-Feb ‘22, we also launched customizable
bounty boards for high-value technical creators, communities and influencers
(currently in invite-only alpha) - we’ve had over 200 signups for our waiting
list, and have already started 5 pilots. * Trecia's Bounty Board (https://twitter.com/TreciaKS female dev, 15k followers
   on Twitter)
 * Pier Paolo's Bounty Board (https://twitter.com/pier_paolo_28?lang=en-GB -
   4.5k followers on Twitter + newsletter and a community)

---
date: 2022-03-21
to: Michelle Roberts <Michelle.Roberts@nttdata.com>
subject: Re: Resignation - Matthew Eisner
msg_id: 17faced0815d1be8
---

Hi Michelle,
In terms of IT equipment, the only thing I have is a laptop which has been
broken for more than 2 months and has forced me to use my own devices at major
inconvenience (including effectively locking me out of multiple NTT accounts).
To use a courier compounds this inconvenience, as I literally just left Europe
today for the US for the foreseeable future, which means that instead of a $20
shipping fee, I will be looking at well over $100, all for a laptop which does
not turn on. All of this seems very silly for a broken device (and like
something that should have been raised much sooner to avoid this situation where
I am being told I must foot much larger bills than necessary due to the late
notice here). Please let me know how you would like to proceed, ideally one that
does not create hundreds of dollars in unnecessary cost. I would be happy to
ship the laptop to an NTT office in the US if that is agreeable.
Also, i do  not have a phone or any other IT equipment - I was told I would be
given a phone at the start of my employment but never was.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-20
to: Kevin Gardner <kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>, Sam Cobbina <Sam@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Vang Advisory annual statement
msg_id: 17fa95f617f7978d
---

Hi Kevin and Sam,

Just following up on my requests around an annual statement for Vang
Capital for calendar year 2021. As I’ve mentioned repeatedly, this is
urgently needed by mid March for my dads tax filings, who does not have the
luxury of extensions as a US resident.

I have accepted I will be late on my filings due to lacking this
information, which now marks our third year in a row missing this deadline,
despite my repeated reminders through the end of 2021 and early 2022
(although I awknowledge not for the past few weeks). I’m really not sure
what to say at this point, except that I need these as fast as possible
now, particularly the Vang Capital, which I imagine is far more complex
than you are anticipating due to generating capital gains, deploying
capital into startups, and cryptocurrency holdings.

Just to repeat, I absolutely need these by April 1 - it will not give my
dads US accountant enough time to process if they are not received by then,
and was a major requirement of our engagement to meet these deadlines, with
this one being by far the most important, as it is each year.

Please let me know how we can proceed ASAP.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-20
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan.fassett@gmail.com>, pawel@techtree.dev
subject: Re: Following up
msg_id: 17fa803be43044f5
---

Hi Ethan,
No problem at all and hope all is going well with you and Glystn!
Much appreciated re: intros - I'm looping in Pawel on cc as he'll likely join on
those intros.
We're also getting close to closing an angel/operator round with effectively no
min ticket at TechTree to bring on people we like and who can help us as we
prepare to go to market/raise from larger funds. I've included a more extended
blurb we've been sending around (as well as links to deck/whitepaper) below. Let
me know if you or anyone you know might be interested in learning more/investing
- we'd love to make a few more inroads in the US ahead of startingfocusing there
a bit more commercially.
All also likely be in  SF in mid April - would love to catch up then!
Best,
Matthew


TechTree  is raising approx. 1-2m USD from top operators, influential groups or
micro-funds in web3. The operator funding round will enable us to strengthen our
team to keep up with existing demand, as well as speed up the execution of our
product roadmap. We'd be looking to get to 100 paying subscription clients
before the end of the year and raise a VC round then.Here'sour deck and here'sa
draft of our white paper outlining our long-term vision of becoming a fully
trustless and transparent social network for tech people, where they can
monetize their personal assets. Since we've launched our beta version in Jan,
we've got an amazingly positive response from all our users - developers,
clients, creators & influencers, VCs and protocols. Here is a summary of our
traction updates from the start of Jan'22:1. Beta launch: We launched our beta
in January and the response from clients has been terrific - 13 new clients in
the last 8 weeks. Previously with our alpha version of our platform in 2021, we
paid 300k+ USD in bounties, had 500k USD in revenue, and partnered with VCs such
as Sequoia, EQT Ventures, and Northzone.
2. Go-to-market ramp up: Additionally, our go-to-market strategy focused on VC
partnerships is working very well, and funds have been super receptive to our
beta version. We’re launching our first 4 VC partnerships in the next month,
have a rapidly growing pipeline, and are in advanced talks with many new
partners. Notion Capital has featured us intheir March newsletter and Technation
introduced us to 31 Series A companies on Friday :)
3. Customizable Bounty Boards: In mid-Feb ‘22, we also launched customizable
bounty boards for high-value technical creators, communities and influencers
(currently in invite-only alpha) - we’ve had over 200 signups for our waiting
list, and have already started 5 pilots. * Trecia's Bounty Board (https://twitter.com/TreciaKS female dev, 15k followers
   on Twitter)
 * Pier Paolo's Bounty Board (https://twitter.com/pier_paolo_28?lang=en-GB -
   4.5k followers on Twitter + newsletter and a community)

date: 2022-03-13
to: sameersingh17@gmail.com, Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Yohann/Sameer intro
msg_id: 17f856888cf48af6
---

Hi Yohann,
Please meet Sameer, who I've known for a while, and is an angel investor, as
well as very  knowledgeable researcher/writer around products with network
effects.
Sameer, please meet Yohann, who I advise a bit on Angelspartners, a matchmaking
platform for startups and angels, and is currently raising a round.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-13
to: Marina Gurevich <marina.gurevich@wintermute-trading.com>
subject: Techtree fundraise - monetization layer and network for devs
msg_id: 17f854df8192844e
---

Hi Marina,
Hope all is well since we last spoke - I ended up taking a full-time role
leading the product and go to  market our web3 offering atTechtree. I hope you
were able to fill your roles, and maybe this email would be better routed to
your new investment director?
We're currently raising a round and looking for strategic investors to bring on,
and I'm reaching out as I wanted to see if you/Wintermute might be interested in
learning more (or/in addition to using our platform to help support portfolio
companies with technical hiring).
To provide a bit more context, at Techtree we are building a platform where
engineers get rewarded for the value they create through their network,
knowledge, and data. We’ve started with recruitment, where our end users have
already received 300k+ USD in bounties by successfully referring tech talent to
jobs at hiring companies.

Here is some more detail onour vision, and a90 or so second video explaining how
our initial product, a SaaS solution for hiring bounties, works.
Since the beta launch of our bounty product in Jan 2022, and we have had some
amazing traction:
 * 10 new companies buying the SaaS subscription for bounties in 7 weeks
 * Over 16 VCs interested in connecting us to the their portcos to offer hiring
   bounties
 * 8 live pilots with communities/creators/influencers and meetups in dev space


Additionally, with an alpha version of our platform in 2021, we paid 300k+ USD
in bounties, had 500k USD in revenue, and partnered with VCs such as Sequoia,
EQT Ventures, and Northzone.

This year we are planning to build and launch the full scale version of our
platform. We received a large grant from one of the top blockchain protocols,
Algorand. This will enable us to transition our platform to web3, which will
allow it to be fully transparent and align our incentive structures.

Our team, while small, includes the experience of founders previously backed by
top VCs, Venture Partners, consultants, and engineers with decades of
experience. We are backed by Frontline Ventures, Notion Capital, and fantastic
angels including the ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday and senior execs from
Stackoverflow, Uber, and Monzo. Here's the link toour pitch deck.

Currently, we are looking to raise a 1-2m USD round from top operators and
select funds, followed by a larger round with larger funds in the near future
- we'd love to chat with you more, provide any additional details, or answer any
questions you might have about our round.

I hope to hear back from you!
Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-13
to: Andrea Bonaceto <andrea@eternacapital.com>
subject: Techtree fundraise info
msg_id: 17f854230db2522f
---

Hey Andrea,
Hope you've been doing well! I just wanted to keep you in the loop on our
upcoming round at Techtree, which I've attached some info on below.
We're currently raising funds from top web3 operators and a few small funds
before raising a larger institutional round. I'd love to have you onboard as an
angel or through Eterna, or let me know if there's anyone you think we should
connect with!
I've cc'ed  Pawel, who can answer deeper questions you might have, and we're
more than happy to jump on a call if you'd like.
Best,
Matthew


We're looking to raise approx. 1-2m USD from top operators, influential groups
or micro-funds in web3. The operator funding round will enable us to strengthen
our team to keep up with existing demand, as well as speed up the execution of
our product roadmap. We'd be looking to get to 100 paying subscription clients
before the end of the year and raise a VC round then.Here'sour deck and here'sa
draft of our white paper outlining our long-term vision of becoming a fully
trustless and transparent social network for tech people, where they can
monetize their personal assets.Since we've launched our beta version in Jan,
we've got an amazingly positive response from all our users - developers,
clients, creators & influencers, VCs and protocols. Here is a summary of our
traction updates from the start of Jan'22:1. Beta version: We launched our beta
in January and the response from clients has been terrific - 7 new clients in
the last 7 weeks. Here are some examples of company bounty boards: * Wayflyer
 * Ramp
   Network


2. Go-to-market ramp up: Additionally, our go-to-market strategy focused on VC
partnerships is working very well, and funds have been super receptive to our
beta version. We’re launching our first 4 VC partnerships in the next month,
have a rapidly growing pipeline, and are in advanced talks with many new
partners. Notion Capital has featured us intheir March newsletter and Technation
introduced us to 31 Series A companies on Friday :)
3. Customizable Bounty Boards: In mid-Feb ‘22, we also launched customizable
bounty boards for high-value technical creators, communities and influencers
(currently in invite-only alpha) - we’ve had over 200 signups for our waiting
list, and have already started 5 pilots. * Trecia's Bounty Board (https://twitter.com/TreciaKS female dev, 15k followers
   on Twitter)
 * Pier Paolo's Bounty Board (https://twitter.com/pier_paolo_28?lang=en-GB -
   4.5k followers on Twitter + newsletter and a community)

(currently in invite-only alpha) - we’ve had over 200 signups for our waiting
list, and have already started 5 pilots. * Trecia's Bounty Board (https://twitter.com/TreciaKS female dev, 15k followers
   on Twitter)
 * Pier Paolo's Bounty Board (https://twitter.com/pier_paolo_28?lang=en-GB -
   4.5k followers on Twitter + newsletter and a community)

As discussed at our last meeting we've now started to raise an operator round.
We're looking to raise approx. 1-2m USD from top operators, influential groups
or micro-funders. The operator funding round would enable us to strengthen the
team to keep up with the demand as well as speed up the execution of our product
roadmap. We'd be hoping to get to 100 paying subscription clients at the end of
the year and raise a VC round then.Here's our deck  and here's a draft of our
white paper  outlining our long-term vision of becoming a fully trustless and
transparent social network for tech people, where they can monetize their
personal assets. Since we've launched our beta version in Jan, we've got an
amazingly positive response from all our users - developers, clients, creators &
influencers, VCs and protocols. Here is a summary of our traction updates from
the start of Jan'22:1. Beta version:  We launched our beta in January and the
response from clients has been terrific - 7 new clients in the last 7 weeks.
Here are some examples of company bounty boards: * Wayflyer
 * Ramp Network


2. Go-to-market ramp up:  Additionally, our go-to-market strategy focused on VC
partnerships is working very well, and funds have been super receptive to our
beta version. We’re launching our first 4 VC partnerships in the next month,
have a rapidly growing pipeline, and are in advanced talks with many new
partners. Notion Capital has featured us in their March newsletter  and
Technation introduced us to 31 Series A companies on Friday :)
3. Customizable Bounty Boards:  In mid-Feb ‘22, we also launched customizable
bounty boards for high-value technical creators, communities and influencers
(currently in invite-only alpha) - we’ve had over 200 signups for our waiting
list, and have already started 5 pilots. * Trecia's Bounty Board (https://twitter.com/TreciaKS female dev, 15k followers
   on Twitter)
 * Pier Paolo's Bounty Board  (https://twitter.com/pier_paolo_28?lang=en-GB -

Hey Josh,
Good to chat last week. Following up on a few points, find below some projects I
have some hands in that might be interesting for you:
Angels Partners - platform to connect fundraising early stage startup  with
angels, has seen quite solid growth over the past few years, recently broke 20k
MRR, founder is currently in London raising a round, also focused on onboarding
accelerators/groups of startups at once.
Artsquare  - marketplace for fractionalize  fine art with Warhol, Banksy, etc. I
had been doing some advisory for them but they're restructuring their marketing
a bit. They generally are looking to connect with anyone who has a high value
art portfolio and might want to put a few interesting pieces on the platform to
fractionalize them/provide liquidity/see if it might spike the price while still
maintaining the physical painting
Oasis Launchgarden - very early project in collaboration between Eterna Capital
and Oasis/ROSE blockchain, basically would be a fund/incubator that Oasis would
try to funnel all projects into, quite a bit of the tech is built but they need
a senior team to launch the company, fundraise, and go to market. I'm trying to
help them place a few senior roles.

Also, I've attached a little blurb about Techtree below, if you (we'd basically
waive a minimum ticket for you) or anyone you know might be interested in the
round I'm working on there.
Let me know if any of the above is of interest!
Best,
Matthew


At Techtree we are building a platform where techies get rewarded for the value
they create through their network, knowledge, and data. We’ve started with
recruitment, where our end users have already received 300k+ USD in bounties by
successfully referring tech talent to jobs at hiring companies. You can find
links toour deck andwhitepaper here as well.

Here is some more detail onour vision, and a90 or so second video explaining how
our initial product, a SaaS solution for hiring bounties, works. Since the beta
launch of our bounty product in Jan 2022, and we have had some amazing traction:
 * 10 new companies buying the SaaS subscription for bounties in 7 weeks
 * Over 16 VCs interested in connecting us to the their portcos to offer hiring
   bounties
 * 8 live pilots with communities/creators/influencers and meetups in dev space

Additionally, with an alpha version of our platform in 2021, we paid 300k+ USD
in bounties, had 500k USD in revenue, and partnered with VCs such as Sequoia,
EQT Ventures, and Northzone.

Hey Gilbert,
I know it's been a while since we've spoke, likely pre-covid, so hope things
have been well with you.
I'm reaching out as I recently jumped on full-time as Head of Product of the
web3/token offering for a company I previously angel invested in, which has been
growing quite rapidly (Techtree). We're raising funding (currently an
operator/angel round, but soon a larger institutional round), and I wanted to
see if it might be something of interest to you/SoftBank.
To provide a bit more context, at Techtree we are building a platform where
techies get rewarded for the value they create through their network, knowledge,
and data. We’ve started with recruitment, where our end users have already
received 300k+ USD in bounties by successfully referring tech talent to jobs at
hiring companies. 
Here is some more detail on our vision, and a 90 or so second video  explaining
how our initial product, a SaaS solution for hiring bounties, works. Since the
beta launch of our bounty product in Jan 2022, and we have had some amazing
traction: * 10 new companies buying the SaaS subscription for bounties in 7
   weeks
 * Over 16 VCs interested in connecting us to the their portcos to
   offer hiring bounties
 * 8 live pilots with communities/creators/influencers and meetups in
   dev space


Additionally, with an alpha version of our platform in 2021, we paid 300k+ USD
in bounties, had 500k USD in revenue, and partnered with VCs such as Sequoia,
EQT Ventures, and Northzone. 
This year we are planning to build and launch the full scale version of our
platform. We received a large grant from one of the top blockchain protocols,
Algorand. This will enable us to transition our platform to web3, which will
allow it to be fully transparent and align our incentive structures.
Our team, while small, includes the experience of founders previously backed by
top VCs, Venture Partners, consultants, and engineers with decades of
experience. We are backed by Frontline Ventures, Notion Capital, and fantastic
angels including the ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday and senior execs from
Stackoverflow, Uber, and Monzo. Here's the link toour pitch deck.
Let me know if you have any initial thoughts, or might want to jump on a call to
learn a bit more about what we're building. I've cc'ed  our CEO, Pawel, who can
answer some more detailed questions if you have any as well.
Best,
Matthew

Hope all is well since we last met up in Florida in early fall. Since we last
spoke, I jumped on to one of my angel investment portfolio companies full time
as Head of Product for our web3/token offering at Techtree, andI wanted to reach
out in regards to our upcoming operator round to see if it might be of interest
to you.
To provide a brief intro, atTechtree we are building a platform where engineers
get rewarded for the help they offer through their network, knowledge, and data.
Here's a bit more detail on ourvision, and a90 or so second video explaining our
beachhead product, a SaaS solution for hiring bounties (which has already paid
out 300k+ USD to our users who have successfully referred technical hires to
companies).
Since the launch of this in Jan 2022, and we have had some amazing traction: * 
   10 new companies buying the SaaS subscription for bounties in 7 weeks
 * 
   Over 16 VCs interested in connecting us to the their portcos to offer hiring
   bounties
 * 8
   live pilots with communities/creators/influencers and meetups in dev space


Additionally, in 2021, we generated 500k USD in revenue and partnered with VCs
such as Sequoia, EQT Ventures, and Northzone. We are planning to scale this up
in the coming year, and have secured a grant from a top blockchains - Algorand -
which will help move our platform to web3, enable us to make it fully
transparent, and allow us to more effectively align our users incentives.
Our team, while small, composes the experience of previously VC backed founders,
Venture Partners, ex-consultants, and engineers with 15+ years of experience. We
are backed by Frontline Ventures, Notion Capital and fantastic angels including
the ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday and senior execs from Stackoverflow, Uber,
and Monzo. Here'sthe link to our pitch deck.
Currently, we are looking to raise 1-2m USD under a SAFE with a 30m USD cap. If
you might want to learn more, I'd love to set up a call with you, myself, and
our CEOPawel (cc'ed). Let me know if this is of interest, or there's  anyone you
think we should connect with!

Matthew

date: 2022-03-06
to: josh@stage.co
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17f604b1fc0cd4bd
---

Hey Josh,
Was great to meet in Denver, and sorry for my delay on this. I've had an
extremely hectic period of time between fundraising for TechTree (which let me
know if you might want some info on the round), some family stuff, and travel
(Ethdenver was quite a week haha).
Please find linked here a brief overview of how I could see working towards a
token sale for Stageverse. I know this is quite late, so no worries at all if
you've moved a different direction, but also would certainly be interested in
discussing further how I might be able to help either way.
Let me know if there might be a good time for a call soon to go over this, and
hope all is well with you!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-06
to: Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>, phillipmckenna99@gmail.com
subject: Phillip/Nassim intro
msg_id: 17f6034006d6ebb6
---

Hi Phillip,

Please meet Nassim, who is a partner and COO for Eterna Capital. I’ve
worked with him on a number of projects over the past few years, and he’s
hoping to connect with people who could help set up a token
accelerator/launchpad for a notable blockchain partner of theirs. He also
has his hands in multiple other projects that might be interesting to you.

Nassim, meet Phillip, who I’ve worked with in the past, and has done very
solid work around crypto/web3 marketing and biz dev (I had him shortlisted
for a role in Vinapa originally until the pivot to focus on aorist). He has
a full time role right now, but I know there are a number of projects that
might be interesting for him you’re working on.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-06
to: Colin Allison <colin.allison92@gmail.com>, Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>
subject: Colin/Nassim intro
msg_id: 17f602dc45c7ee1e
---

Hey Colin,

Please meet Nassim, who is a partner and COO for Eterna Capital. I’ve
worked with him on a number of projects over the past few years, and he’s
hoping to connect with people around a potential accelerator/token
launchpads they might set up with a notable blockchain (as well as
potentially other projects).

Nassim, please meet Colin, who I worked with at Startupbootcamp, and has
since been running accelerators (including for Facebook) and overseeing the
venture building/startup studio practice for Rainmaking in APAC. He also
manages some family and friends money in defi, and is looking to jump full
time in to web3.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-06
to: jelmer posthumus <jelmerposthumus@hotmail.com>
subject: Valencia on the 10th
msg_id: 17f5ffef9d1559a6
---

Hey Jelmer,
Was great to chat the other day - sounds like you're working on some interesting
stuff and will be keen to hear how it progresses.
It looks like I'll be in Valencia the  evening of the 10th on my way from
Barcelona to Malaga. Want to maybe grab dinner (my go to would be paella on the
boardwalk, but I'm up for whatever)?
Let me know if that might work for you!
Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-06
to: Guillaume Agis <guillaume.agis@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Rhapsody : Engage with artists & live extraordinary experiences
msg_id: 17f5f83c2812028b
---

Hey man,
Was great to catch up, and hope we get to hang out in person soon :)
So this sounds cool, and would  love to help however I can. Re: our messages
earlier, happy to be a bit of a mentor around marketing/growth for you,
something like a call every few weeks to catch up and discuss where you are/go
to market. And there are a couple of other people I could connect you with
around that. That being said, you might actually find some very good contacts
via growthmentor, so would definitely encourage you to check that out. And
either way please do keep me in the loop on progress/if you're rasing  funds!
A few thoughts come to mind: * Your dream music advisor/mentor would likely be
   someone like RAC or 3lau, but maybe slightly smaller.
 * I have a friend in Atlanta  who is working on something a little similar,
   although I think more of a sort of bidding to curate/produce live experiences
    model. His weak points are the tech, and his strong points are
   partnerships/marketing/biz dev (and he has some significant connections in
   the US music industry). Happy to intro  you if you'd like.


On my side, we're moving forward pretty quickly with a friends/operator round at
TechTree before we go into institutional fundraising at likely a much higher
valuation. If you might be interested in putting some money in (or just using
TechTree to monetize some of the talent referrals you make/do some referral
based hiring), I'd be more than happy to connect you with either our CEO or Head
of Partnerships who can tell you a bit more about either of those paths. I've
pasted a bit of info below - any feedback is also greatly appreciated!
Best,
Matthew


At  Techtree  we are building a platform where techies get rewarded for the help
they offer through their network, knowledge and data. Here is further input on
our  vision, and our initial product to reach this is a SaaS solution for hiring
bounties. You can find a link to a rough draft of our whitepaper here, and here
is a  96 sec video  explaining how hiring bounties work. Since the launch of
this in Jan 2022, and we have had some amazing traction: * 10 new companies
   buying the SaaS subscription for bounties in 7 weeks
   
   
 * 15+ VCs interested in
   connecting us to the their portfolio companies to offer hiring bounties
   
   


It is also important to mention that last year we paid over 300k USD in bounties
in our alpha version of the product, had 500k USD in revenue and partnered with
VCs such as Sequoia, EQT Ventures, and Northzone. This year we are planning to
take this to the next level and got a grant from one of the top blockchain
protocols - ALGO - which will help move our platform to web 3.0. to make it
fully transparent and align our users incentives. Our team, while small,
composes the experience of previously VC backed founders, Venture Partners,
ex-consultants and engineers with 15+ years of experience. We are backed by
Frontline Ventures, Notion Capital and fantastic angels including the ex CEO of
Skype, CTO of Workday and senior execs from Stackoverflow, Uber, and Monzo.
Here's the link to our pitch deck.
Currently, we are looking to raise a 1-2m USD round from top operators at a 30m
USD pre-money valuation.

subject: Josh/Laurence Intro
msg_id: 17f59811c133d4e2
---

I've mentioned to both of you this intro was coming, so will keep it short.
Josh, I've worked with Laurence across a couple of companies where he's been
able to secure some pretty impressive partnerships. He's a co-founder on
TechTree, and manages our partnerships and biz dev, so is would  be the best
person to talk to on that front.
Laurence, Josh  is pretty incredible at setting up partnerships, and did quite a
bit setting up some major ones in the talent/recruitment space with Wiser (as
well as some very notable global brands), as well as brought Pawel into Antler
as a venture partner. He's looking to take on another project or two, and I
think it'd be great if we could find a way to align.
I'll let you take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

subject: Catching up in Spain
