Hey Ultan,
Hope all is well, and sorry for my delay in following up on our last convo.
We've been in a bit of a heads down mode at TechTree, and are just coming up to
really ramp up our fundraising. There are also a few other projects I have some
hands in I'd love to chat to you about. I'll send you a bit of a longer email on
that later today/over the weekend.
But more immediately, I just got off a call with Josh who mentioned you're in
south  of Spain. I'm in Madrid for the next 3-4 days, and then heading to
Valencia/Malaga for a week or so until going to Portugal for a week. It'd be
great to grab lunch/coffee/a drink if you might have some time towards the end
of next week.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-04
to: Brian Nichols <brian@hustlefundvc.com>
subject: Re: Limited Spots: Free trial of Angel Squad (invest w/ Hustle Fund!)
msg_id: 17f524ddd5ed17bb
---

Hi Brian,
Sounds interesting. I'd be happy to join (I'm in). Looks like y'all also
followed me into my investment in Yummy, so seems  like there maybe is a wider
conversation here as well around deal flow :)
I'm also currently raising a round for https://techtree.dev if that might be of
interest to you. We've seen huge growth in the past year, and are about to
launch our web3 product and token soon. A big use case of ours is also helping
VCs provide value to their portfolios by creating networks of engineering talent
to refer into and across their companies, so maybe that's of interest (we're
working with Seedcamp, Eterna Capital, Notion, and quite a few other funds
around this).
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-03-04
to: Ryan Donnelly <ryandonz@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Catching up and cheqd
msg_id: 17f52496d91cf2a4
---

Hey man,
Sorry for the delay on my side now. Sounds like quite a few months you've been
having.
Re: TechTree - I've pasted a bit of a boiler plate  blurb we've been sending
below that gives an overview (as well as links to our very rough v1
whitepaper).
The thinking as to why token is 1) transparency, 2) escrow for successful talent
placements until the hire's trial period is done, 3) to provide liquidity to
another token layer we're working on (sort of social token/monetization stream
for engineers/communities for lack of a better one sentence description), and 4)
to use to incentivize acquisition/onboarding/engagement to end users.
In terms of liquidity and LPs, we'll likely be requiring a liquidity commitment
of a few multiples of funding from the later VCs we bring in to the second
tranche of the round (which seems to be becoming a bit of a norm from what I'm
seeing, although curious your take on this). We'll look to provide exit
liquidity for early investors to recover a few multiples of their principal via
something like unlocked staking rewards (similar to FLOW) or lp farming, but are
planning to have pretty long lockup periods. We're also in talks with CoinList
about maybe doing a sale on their platform, as well as a few market makers
(Wintermute, FalconX, etc). And we'll be reserving the majority of the token
supply for platform incentivization. We're also in talks with a fair amount of
defi protocols about incentivizing DEX liquidity pools, getting pairs on yield
aggregators, getting listed on various sites, etc. We'll likely ramp this up
heavily when we're a little farther in the fundraise.
Would love your feedback though (positive or negative) :)
Re: cheqd, I'll plan to hold, so no huge rush on the distribution, but would  be
good to get that sorted out just so I know what to expect there. Definitely
makes sense re: waiting for identity narrative, and I'm starting to hear some
murmurings in some strange places (enterprise consulting, crypto lawyers, etc)
about identity as an upcoming frontier.
I'm in Spain for the next 3 weeks or so, but am quite flexible, so let me know
some times that might work for you (or feel free to send me a cal  invite and we
can shuffle if needed, or a calendly link if you use that).
Let me know when might  work for you!
Best,
Matthew




I’m reaching out because after launching our beta in January we’ve had amazing
traction, and we’re now looking to raise approx. 1-2m USD from top operators. It
would be great to have you on board in a personal capacity as I believe you can
help us a lot when it comes to figuring out our web 3.0 approach.
The operator funding round would enable us to strengthen the team to keep up
with the demand as well as speed up the execution of our product roadmap. We'd
be hoping to get to 100+ paying subscription clients at the end of the year and
raise a VC round then.Here's our deck  and here's the white paper  outlining our
long-term vision of becoming a fully trustless and transparent web3 social
network for tech people, where they can monetize their personal assets (network,
knowledge, and data). Feel free to share it along with this update with anyone
you believe could be a good fit.Since we've launched our beta version in Jan,
we've got an amazingly positive response from all our users - developers,
clients, creators & influencers, VCs, and protocols.Below is a summary of our
traction  from the start of Jan'22. Looking forward to hearing from you!

Matt mentioned that you'd be interested in potentially participating in such
round, so let's catchup! 1. Beta launch: We launched our beta version in January and the response from
    clients has been terrific - 7 new clients in the last 8 weeks. Here are some
    examples of company bounty boards:
    
    

 * Wayflyer
   
   
 * Ramp Network
   
   

 1. Go-to-market ramp up: Additionally, our go-to-market strategy based on VC
    partnerships is also working very well and they have been super receptive to
    our beta version. We’re launching our first 4 VC partnerships in the next
    month and have a quite advanced pipeline with many new partners.
    
    


VC PartnershipsNotion Capital has featured us  in their March newsletter  and
Technation has introduced us to 31 Series A companies on Friday :) 1. 
    Customizable Bounty Boards: In mid-Feb we’ve also launched customizable
    bounty boards for creators, communities and influencers -> we’ve had over
    200 such people on the waiting list and we’ve started 5 pilots already.
    
    
    
    

Some examples of the customized creator boards are here:- Trecia's Bounty Board 
(https://twitter.com/TreciaKS  female dev, 15k followers on Twitter) - Pier
Paolo's Bounty Board  (https://twitter.com/pier_paolo_28?lang=en-GB  - 4.5k
followers on Twitter + newsletter and a community)

Hi Evan,
Please meet Nassim, who is a founder and partner at Eterna Capital. I've worked
with them for a few years on a couple of projects now, and have always been very
impressed with their approach to investing in the space. They backed and have
their hands in quite a few interesting blockchains and protocols, and simply
following some of their investments has given me quite a bit of alpha.
Nassim, please meet Evan, who is currently leads  the crypto/blockchain practice
for a prominent global law firm, but is interviewing a bunch  of places (most of
which are name brands within the space) to jump into a GC or similar role full
time in web3. He's also very bullish on Oasis and had actually mentioned the 
launchgarden  to me at Ethdenver  before we had our call on it :)
I'll let you two take it from here!
Matthew

date: 2022-03-01
to: Nick Bucheleres <nick@smilecoin.us>
subject: Re: FalconX < > Smile Coin, Market Making Agreement
msg_id: 17f449f68b138501
---

So this generally looks reasonable to me - I didn’t realize at first this
was just market making and not also an exchange listing. I assume they’ve
worked with some other erreurs le protocols?

On the fees front, I feel like you’d know more about how the economics of
this look re payment based on some metric related to strike price set by
TWAP but I imagine how most of these deals are done. Is there no up front
flat fee? That was definitely common back in the ICO wave in 17-18, often
in the 6 figure range. I also didn’t really get from the contract what
happens if they don’t actually improve the price of the token

Also it doesn’t look like they’ve included the discount on the 3.5% fee
they mentioned in their proposal in the contract. There wasn’t anything
particularly crazy looking to me in the contract, but I skimmed and obvs
not a lawyer.

Will try to join the call tomorrow to wallflower connection at JFK
permitting

date: 2022-02-25

someone to help with social marketing, and thought it might be interesting for

for.

Best,
Matthew

Halina, please meet Andrea, who is a founder of Eterna Capital and more recently

---
date: 2022-02-25
to: Melissa Smith <melissa@thepva.com>
subject: Re: Would you feel comfortable giving me career advice via email?
msg_id: 17f2e3e0dce196e1
---

Hi Melissa,
This sounds interesting. I took a poke around the site and through their
whitepaper, and certainly digital twinning is a big use case I see in blockchain
long term.
In terms of Switzerland and Dubai, both are generally pretty friendly to
business interests, and particularly crypto. That being said, you should
certainly make sure you're getting paid in the US, as it will likely
complicate your filings and raise your taxes to be paid abroad.
The bigger questions  to me on this is more on the product side of things. The
advisors and senior team seem impressive, but I don't see very much depth on the
blockchain side, which is a bit of a red flag for me. But my default is to be
very skeptical in the blockchain space, as a huge portion of it is scammy. I
guess the big question that you should by trying  to get an answer to without
actually asking is if the hype dies, crypto prices crash, and we go into another
deep bear market, will they keep building for those 2 years to bring the product
to market.
Let me know if these points raise any other questions, or if there's anything
else you'd like to bounce off me as you're going through that process.
Hope all is well!
Matthew

---
date: 2022-02-24
to: Darcie Tanner <darcie@makk.co>
subject: Re: Nova Liquidity Mining program
msg_id: 17f2b3daf8a1b302
---

Hey, sure, will include some ramblings below. Also my dates for Europe are
solid now - mar 1-21, so would be good to maybe have a few catch-ups then
to transition to a more advisory role and make sure I’m aligning in the
right ways (and find a time to meet Tom when I’m in PT)

Sorry I haven’t been keeping up on our late night calls as well - if I’m
being overly honest, I’m trying to establish a bit of a social life post
Covid and 6 years before that of most of my social life being through work
(which is basically out the window in this post Covid remote world), as
well as had a pair of deaths in the family (not untimely or unexpected,
both were 90+ years old), my own bout with what was likely Covid, and a
crazy weekend at eth Denver. That’s certainly no excuse for me being less
than adequately responsive, so my apologies

1 and 2 are sort of blurred in this space. I’m just going to put some
bulleted ideas below:
-they should be looking to ensure platforms like Defillama, pools.fyi, and
other defi yield listing sites are listing their pools/staking programs -
I’m not 100% sure what the ones that are most prevalent in the solana
ecosystem, but defillama definitely at least is one of them (and there are
a slew of others I’m not mentioning just because they don’t track solana
defi protocols)
-they should definitely be looking to get cross promotion reach through
their launchpad and any other existing partners. Especially if there is a
pure nova for nova staking program (see nova for protocol revenue point
immediately below), as this basically means any early token holders would
be losing money by not staking
-it would help to know a bit more detail about the program. Is this more
like a Beethoven on Fantom or Balancer on various chains program where you
just have to stake other asset combinations in or use the protocol, or is
there a way to just stake Nova tokens for protocol revenue, or both? I
realize this may be a lot of jargon for you, so let me know if you want me
to elaborate
-they should certainly activate any email lists and
discord/telegram/Twitter around this. Depending on their rewards model,
holders of nova token may be liable to just haemorrhage money if they don’t
stake, which could create some ill will - see above points)
-also an important question - what partners are facilitating this liquidity
mining. Solana is frankly one of the chains I’m less familiar with,
although I have been trying to use it recently just to gain some context
for this project. Are there particular decentralised exchanges (DEXs) that
they will be conducting this program through? Is nova technically a dex
itself, or are they looking to execute this program through their own
protocol? If they’re looking to partner with a solana dex (maybe soldex I
think is a major one?), which one is it, and they should be looking to
cross promote through them as much as possible and facilitate that partner
marketing. If it’s their own dex, they may want to look at partnering with
yield aggregators (beefy finance is probably the most notable, except one
of the few chains it doesn’t cover is solana, and I’m not sure what the
solana yield aggregators are, but I’d bet they’d at least have heard of
beefy and know what the solana equivalent is. I can also look it up on
defillama, but I’m not at my computer)


Also, a few other points that I think we should maybe raise with
Paddy/decide how we can bring forward under a mutually beneficial
commercial agreement to Nova:
-I know a few blockchains giving out very large grants for protocols
willing to build on them, and if Nova has a desire to go cross chain, this
could be interesting to them maybe. This is basically 6+ figure equity-free
funding, and frankly the easiest capital I’ve ever seen in 10 years in
startups. Also a few funds I could intro to
-I have a friend (James Palmer and Vincent Dignan/Vin Clancy if you were
ever into the London Startups FB group) who runs an influencer marketing
agency that claims to be able to pump tokens 30%+ of their market cap, sell
out large pre-sales, and launch $250k+ Solana NFT mints using their lists
and messaging tactics. They don’t get out of bed for more than $50k
generally, but are working with some close friends on mine at Grin
Gaming/SMILE Coin on a campaign. If that goes well, I might highly
recommend this, and regardless this would be the most clear path to
scalable/repeatable/measurable influencer marketing in my eyes  (despite
the fact I was always skeptical of Vincent’s legitimacy)

Hopefully this is useful, and happy to elaborate on any points,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-02-23
to: amit@borderlesscapital.io
subject: Catching up/potential intro
msg_id: 17f28970a5771dd9
---

Hi Amit,
Hope all is well with you since we last spoke during the AEA program. I was
hanging out with Kai from Webblen in Denver, and he spoke very highly of his
conversations with Borderless.
I wanted to reach out as I've joined https://techtree.dev/ full-time (which I
set up with Addie a while back for a grant, and will be largely built on
Algorand) to lead their web3 launch/token distribution. I'd love to jump on a
call with you and our CEO to pick your brain a bit around people we should be
talking to in the Algorand ecosystem, as well as hear any tips you might have as
we bring the token and web 3 product to market.
Also, if you might be interested, one of my good friends from college and
neighbors in Los Angeles is Nick Bucheleres from Grin Gaming/SMILE Coin - I'm
not sure if you're connected with them, but happy to make an intro if not.
Sounds like they're about to go into their A round fundraise.
Let me know if there might be some times that work for you to catch up in the
coming weeks!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-02-17
to: Darcie Tanner <darcie@makk.co>
subject: Re: Nova ongoing
msg_id: 17f05b2e8a7fd2c7
---

Hi Darcie,

This all makes sense to me, and also generally I want to give my apologies
for being a little less engaged than I’d like to be. I’ve been balancing my
normal workload on top of a bout of (still not confirmed but likely) Covid,
a death in the family, and onboarding to a new major role (which maybe
could turn into a Makk project) - of course none of these excuse me being a
bit MIA.

Ad hoc support sounds more than doable for me, and maybe something that can
scale a bit better across multiple projects (particularly as I think you
generally pick up very quickly on some of the nuances of web3 vs web2
marketing).

I’d love to meet up with Tom in Portugal, and would also be very keen to
discuss with you and Paddy how we might work in some of the more
advisory-ish value I can add to some of Makk’s clients (grant funding,
influencer agency intros, other high value connections).

And it also might be good to have a call to align in general. As you can
imagine, Denver is a bit hectic for me this week, but maybe sometime middle
of next?

One thought is I would probably hope to up my rate a little bit (or have
some sort of path to invest in the companies we work with) given what I’m
generally charging, but I can still be a bit flexible on this. And if these
sorts of agreements with tokens might be a bit out of Makk’s depth, I’d be
happy to help figure out some engagement structures that could allow for
this (as I generally think investing in any project that has a serious
roadmap of 6-12 months and a tram of real humans is usually a good ROI in
this space).

Hope all is well, an let me know when might make sense to catch up,

Matthew

---
date: 2022-02-17
to: anais@techtree.dev
subject: Fwd: Stageverse Deck
msg_id: 17f05ac1608ad2d3
---

Not sure if this is helpful, but a very high level deck around web3
community building and discord one of my friends put together - we could
likely contract him (under some very rigid terms as he can be a little
flakey) to configure and moderate our discord a bit (likely for payment in
tokens)

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Kevin Meyer <kfm730@gmail.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Feb 2022 at 15:17
Subject: Stageverse Deck
To: Matt Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>


Remember - this is super high level for conversation more than anything but
it is attached. I would gladly make one etc for TechTree specifically that
can be more in depth etc.

---
date: 2022-02-17
to: Marko Srsan <marko.srsan@memgraph.io>
subject: Re: Memgraph - ahead of the chat tomorrow
msg_id: 17f05aab73f4774f
---

Hey Marko,

Good to hear from you, and no worries re: hiring for the growth role. I’m
still very interested in what you’re working on, and would like to get
involved if possible.

That being said, I’ve gone down the rabbit hole so to speak, and have taken
on a role leading the web3 and token launch for https://Techtree.dev, a
company I invested in in its preseed and is scaling and spinning out a
blockchain platform. We have a big angle on monetising engineering content,
resources, and more generally value, which might be an interesting
ecosystem for you to engage with.

I’ll potentially be in London in early March, or at the very least in
Europe (definitely Portugal and Spain for most of March), and would love to
catch up then, or for a call in the coming weeks.

Let me know what makes sense for you!

Matthew

Hi Meagan,
Marius worked with me at Startupbootcamp in an operations role (not directly
reporting to me) and for Algorand Europe Accelerator managing back office
operations and our database (directly reporting to me).
His performance in the roles were both very good, and he did an excellent job
maintaining and improving high value data systems that were essential to our
operations. At AEA, this database was actually built on Airtable. This
enabled us to scout thousands of blockchain companies and drive nearly 200
applications to our first-time accelerator program.
I'd like to see Mariusz develop more management skills, as i could  see him
moving into a role teaching and overseeing others if he can get his confidence
up a little. I think his peers would generally describe him as pleasant to work
with and thorough in his work. Mariusz will probably have a slight adjustment
period in starting a new role before he is really able to take on tasks
autonomously with confidence.
Mariusz left AEA after the program cycle ended as per our initial plan when
hiring him. I would certainly hire him again.
Best,
Matthew

end for us to sign and show the repayment. Is this something Shield can help

and looking to do the same for Vang Capital.

date: 2022-02-03
to: Ultan Miller <ultan.miller@saxonadvisors.com>
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17ec19816c53a923
---

Hey Ultan,
Sorry for my delay in following up - I've been a bit under the weather this
week, so not as on top of email as usual. Was great to catch up the other week,
and sounds like some impressive projects you've been working on.
Our CEO, Pawel, is currently on holiday, but I'll look to intro  you next week.
In the meantime, I've attached a bit more info about TechTree. The whitepaper is
still very WIP, so don't share that yet, but I'd love any thoughts or feedback,
as well as to connect with anyone you think might be interesting.
And let me know if there's anything I'm forgetting or that I can help with that
you're working on!
Best,
Matthew


Whitepaper:https://docs.techtree.dev/whitepaper/

Explainer video:Loom | Send a video. Not a thousand words.

Get your message across with instantly shareable videos for work. Capture your
screen, record your front-facing camera, and narrate it all at once, then
instantly…

LOOM.COM

How we can help as TechTree (generally):
As TechTree we’re building tools for communities and people like you to help
them get:a) recurring revenue from their network (companies who want to engage
with your network)b) transactional revenue (significant) if you help them with
various things (we’re starting from hiring as the fees are very high)
So far our users have earned over 300k USD from our clients - we have several
who earned over 50k USD in less than a year.
What is important is that as TechTree we’re not sponsoring communities - we’re
building software they can use to engage their network and give their users a
chance to earn money.


Here’s how you can use TechTree (specifically) - and have a pretty quick return
from investment with almost no effort:
1. Recommend people yourself.
You can see examples of the active roles - most are remote, open for candidates
from entire Europe. Even if people are not hired by the companies to which
you’ll recommend them, you will get paid if they’re hired later.

2. Recurring revenue from companies who want to post bounties.Companies from
your network could pay 200 GBP (normal price is 500 GBP) for posting an
unlimited amount of roles with bounties on TechTree. These roles are then
promoted by various TechTree communities (and among our users). * You would get
   50% of every subscription.
 * All it takes
   is to ask your members to ask the companies they work for to post bounties.
   200 GBP per month is cheaper than any job board.
 * 7 companies
   subscribing for a year gives you over 10k EUR in annual subscription fee and
   you don’t need to do anything for the sponsor, because everything is handled
   by us and our team.


3. Enable your members to unlock the value from their networkYour members will
be able to recommend people and get bounty payments. Every time they’re
successful you earn 25% of what they earn. So if your members get 4 bounties for
10k EUR each, you get paid 2500 EUR per each of them (so 10 k EUR) in total.The
roles on our platform are coming only from top VC backed companies (Index,
Sequoia, Northzone, Localgloble, Seedcamp and many more top global VCs)

---
date: 2022-02-03
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan.fassett@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Following up
msg_id: 17ec19377ce95b42
---

Hi Ethan,
Hope all is well, and Pawel tells me you two had a very good conversation :)
For SKALE, Ledger, and Stronghold,
At TechTree, we're building tools for developers, communities, and companies to
help them get:a) recurring revenue from their network (companies who want to
engage with engineer networks and communities)b) transactional revenue
(significant) for individuals or communities who add value to our engineer
network (we’re starting with technical hiring as the fees are very high)
We've validated our web2 product and our users have earned over 300k USD from
our clients - we have several who earned over 50k USD in less than a year. We're
planning our web3 product launch and token sale/distribution for the coming
months, and are in the process of connecting with partners and communities that
might benefit from our platform and help us as we go to market.

For Eniac:
At TechTree, we're building tools for developers, communities, and companies to
help them get:a) recurring revenue from their network (companies who want to
engage with engineer networks and communities)b) transactional revenue
(significant) for individuals, communities, or portfolios who add value to our
engineer network (we’re starting with technical hiring as the fees are very
high)
We've validated our web2 product and our users have earned over 300k USD from
our clients - we have several who earned over 50k USD in less than a year. We're
currently finalizing our lead investor and planning subsequent token sale
rounds, and are essentially oversubscribed, but looking to find the best
strategic fits for us as we go to market with our web3 platform.

If it's helpful for either, please feel free to send them this video too
- https://www.loom.com/share/c0d9f5ea297d4dd5bbafe17c026a6df9
And we're almost done with our v1 version of our whitepaper - I'd certainly love
any thoughts or feedback as we polish it up for initial distribution -
https://docs.techtree.dev/whitepaper/


And  would be great to connect with Strada and more  than happy to give you my
thoughts.
I'm hoping to be up in SF again for a week in mid March, and then likely for
longer in late spring/early summer, so will definitely let you know. And always
happy to catch up on a call if there's anything you're working on I can help
with!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-02-03
to: dave@ockams.com, stas@techtree.dev
subject: David/Stas intro
msg_id: 17ec1741213eadd9
---

Hi David,
Please meet Stas, who I mentioned is leading our blockchain development for
TechTree - he has a very interesting background in data, engineering, and
investment, and has been building on blockchains for 5+ years, and currently on
Algorand.
Stas, please meet David, who was in the accelerator cohort I ran for Algorand,
and is working on some very interesting cross chain infrastructure. He's was 
also one of the most experienced developers in our program in terms of
exposure to Algorand.
I think you two might be a useful resource for each other to bounce ideas off
of. Also David, maybe worth it for you to join our TechTree discord? Still very
much a WIP, but we'll be setting it up and launching it in the coming month or
so. https://discord.gg/6pP7Dc5c
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-02-03
to: Thomas Chen <tom@magic.fund>
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17ec16e2957a25c2
---

Hi Tom,
Sorry for my delay in following up - I've been a bit under the weather the past
week or so, so a bit less on top of email than normal.
Following up from our conversation, please find below a bit more information
about what we're building/our value prop at TechTree. I've also included a very
rough v0.1 version of our whitepaper, but will send you an updated draft when we
wrap up the v1 in the coming weeks.
I'd certainly love any thoughts or feedback you have, or to connect with anyone
you think might be interesting for us to talk to. And If you're interested in
learning a bit more or setting up a developer community on the platform, I'd be
happy to intro  you to our CEO, Pawel, who can give you a bit more detail.
And would love to join your Discord next month if there's room!
Really enjoyed our conversation, and hope to catch up soon (I'm traveling the
next few weeks, but maybe a coffee/bite/drink when I'm back in LA in March?).
Best,
Matthew





Whitepaper: https://docs.techtree.dev/whitepaper/

Explainer video:  Loom | Send a video. Not a thousand words.

Get your message across with instantly shareable videos for work. Capture your
screen, record your front-facing camera, and narrate it all at once, then
instantly…

loom.com  

How we can help as TechTree (generally):
As TechTree we’re building tools for communities and people like you to help
them get:a) recurring revenue from their network (companies who want to engage
with your network)b) transactional revenue (significant) if you help them with
various things (we’re starting from hiring as the fees are very high)
So far our users have earned over 300k USD from our clients - we have several
who earned over 50k USD in less than a year.
What is important is that as TechTree we’re not sponsoring communities - we’re
building software they can use to engage their network and give their users a
chance to earn money. 


Here’s how you can use TechTree (specifically)  - and have a pretty quick return
from investment with almost no effort:
1. Recommend people yourself.
You can see examples of the active roles - most are remote, open for candidates
from entire Europe. Even if people are not hired by the companies to which
you’ll recommend them, you will get paid if they’re hired later.

2. Recurring revenue from companies who want to post bounties. Companies from
your network could pay 200 GBP (normal price is 500 GBP) for posting an
unlimited amount of roles with bounties on TechTree. These roles are then
promoted by various TechTree communities (and among our users). * You would get
   50% of every subscription.
 * All it takes
   is to ask your members to ask the companies they work for to post bounties.
   200 GBP per month is cheaper than any job board.
 * 7 companies
   subscribing for a year gives you over 10k EUR in annual subscription fee and
   you don’t need to do anything for the sponsor, because everything is handled
   by us and our team. 


3. Enable your members to unlock the value from their networkYour members will
be able to recommend people and get bounty payments. Every time they’re
successful you earn 25% of what they earn. So if your members get 4 bounties for
10k EUR each, you get paid 2500 EUR per each of them (so 10 k EUR) in total. The
roles on our platform are coming only from top VC backed companies (Index,
Sequoia, Northzone, Localgloble, Seedcamp and many more top global VCs)

date: 2022-02-02
to: Patrick Sheehan <psheehan007@icloud.com>
subject: Re: Catch up
msg_id: 17ebc4c44ea59c3c
---

Hi Pat,
Thanks for reaching out, and would love to catch up again. I was actually
drafting a response to your message last night that I've pasted below.
(referencing the chartbook article) My initial take is this is the first well
thought out criticism of bitcoin and cryptocurrencies I've heard in a long time.
That being said, to me it's more a criticism of bitcoin, particularly in terms
of the conservative/anarchist/liberetarian points, as opposed to of
decentralized  applications, smart contracts, decentralized finance, DAOs, etc.
They do seem to attempt to acknowledge these areas and blanket them in, but I'm
not sure they fully understand what these will likely look like in the long term
from what I'm reading. But I also certainly have a somewhat biased take here.
Also, would it be interesting for you to connect with this founder? He's who I
first worked with in tech in SF and working on a game testing/QA marketplace
beachhead into ML/AI for testing product that seems to be running away a bit in
terms of pre launch funding and traction -https://www.linkedin.com/in/idanbeck/

Re: Zapier,/Wade Foster, I'm a big fan, and quite a heavy Zapier user (although
these days am not  so hands on with  the actual implementation).
I'll read over the research, will  be very curious - and looking forward to talk 
 more on web3/defi. Let me know some times in the coming week or two that might
be good for you!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2022-02-01
to: Josh Abbott <joshthomasabbott@gmail.com>
subject: techtree info
msg_id: 17eb714487095e77
---

About TechTree:A social network where developers help each other to level up
while getting rewarded for the help they offer. Initially, we’re helping
engineers to help each other find other devs to work with and getting paid the
same amount of money as recruiters for it (over 10k USD per successful
recommendation). We have already paid our users over 300k USD in bounties for
such activities with the biggest single hire being worth 45k USD. 

Our partners and clients are some of the biggest VCs and tech  companies in the
world like: Seedcamp, Local Globe, Northzone or Personio, Tessian, Advent, and
many more.
We’re backed by some amazing investors like Frontline Ventures, Notion Capital,
and fantastic angels including ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday, senior execs
from Stackoverflow, Uber, Monzo and many more. We’re a 15 people  team doing
slightly over 50k USD monthly revenue.
Here’s some more information about us:
 1. Deck
    
    
 2. Light paper:
    
    
 3. Blog - why we decided to build TechTree:

like I must have closed my computer before the send completed.

I’ve been a bit under the weather the past day or two, hoping not Covid.
Nothing to bad in terms of symptoms, but decidedly take today and tomorrow
off to recover.

Is there any chance we can reschedule for sometime next week?

Best,

Matthew

Hi David,
Hope all is well with you and your new year has started well.
I did some work with a company a while back called Folks Finance, a
blockchain/defi which is looking to set up operations including incorporation,
employment agreements, benefits, etc in the UK. I was curious if you might be
able to help them or know someone who could?
Would also be great to catch up sometime. i'm on the west coast these days, but
hopefully should be back in the UK in spring or over the summer.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-01-26
to: Darcie Tanner <darcie@makk.co>
subject: Re: Customer journey matrix & Strategy update
msg_id: 17e94d6df0d9a615
---

Hi Darcie,
I'm not sure the best way to feed in notes on this, so I've collected my
thoughts expanding on the bullets you sent below.
For Discord, I would leave it on there, but just that we probably should look at
it in terms of growing and keeping the Nova discord active as opposed to
targeting other discords to market through (althogh I do think that probably
comes in to a wider influencer strategy, although almost all of those
influencers will be more visible on twitter)
Let me know if there's a better way to get you the below info, or if it'd be
helpful for me to look over things when you compile them (or us to have a
sidebar call around that).
Hope all is well!
Matthew


-   SEO (mini audit & recommendations), next steps- quick seo audit - Url
hierarchy creation- Keyword analysis- Title and description tag speccing-
Technical and non technical roadmapping and recommendations - Backlink creation
strategy and directory listing- Maintenance process creation and handoff- 
 Crypto expert audience (anything they are missing at the moment or we should
look to do/be at on in  the next6-12 months- I would say this should be a
peripheral focus/other area. It's a very hit or miss strategy that can be very
effective if it works, but is very hard to execute- the caveat to this might be
any experts particularly in the solana ecosystem may be good to target. Still I
think most of this can be included as part of a wider influencer strategy- one
other are to maybe consider under this unbrellas is advisors - I have some
interesting contacts, but not sure how that falls under our purview or how we'd
handle introing people as part of our work
-   Influencer strategy - from macro to larger relationships - I think growing
outside their niche to get some 'normal' people involved in the product will be
crucial - we will likely need to do a fair bit of brainstorming here with Nova
to determine what they see as the easiest paths to entry for their product,
including onramps from exchanges or onchainn  to allow new users to get funds
onto solana chain, and then how to use Nova. Then we'll have to think about who
fits best with that (and likely have to try and establish some deeper
partnerships to support that accessibility). I do think there is a narrower
influencer strategy of targeting influencers who have reach with relatively new
solana users (which is a relatively large portion of new crypto users coming on
chain to use defi) - there are a lot of these on twitter, discord, etc-
brainstorming of goals, value propositions, budget, and strategy- Initial pilot
campaign planning- Pilot campaign execution oversight- planning of influencer
program scaling
-   Considerations for ecouraging  current Nova fans into people who create
portfolios (maybe this falls under content themes)- to me this falls into wider
tutorials, guides, videos, etc. There could be contests or other programs to
incentivise engagement here though. The most surefire way of encouraging this is
through announcing token rewards/airdrops for early adopters/users
-   Any content theme thoughts and ideas. What content should go where (even if
just from an expert perspective)- I think tutorial content is probably the most
essential form of content, then we'd probably want to look at more thought
leadership and technical explainers. "How Nova is different" content  could also
be very good, as well as anything that brings transparency to the team/roadmap.
Monthly update calls that the community can join are relatively commonn  in this
space as well-   Your thoughts on measurement -  Total value locked in protocol
and active wallet are huge drivers here- Most crypto companies do a poor job of
acquisition funnel tracking, although this is particularly tricky given the norm
of browser level ad blockers in the space. This might be somewhere to see what
metrics are available, start with an initial kpi benchmark, and then look at how
we can create more comprehensive coverage. I do think this would be very helpful
 -   What are some titles we should aim to be seen in terms of blogs, press etc-
the most common are announcements for number of unique users and milestones in
total locked value - Partnerships are also another big area here- Apart from
those types of announcements, useful topics might be: ADD TOPIcS-   Thoughts on
email part of the user journey- I think the first step here is probably making
sure they embed some sort of email hook as well as useful magnet (early access,
VIP updates, airdrops, etc) to attract sign ups - when the actual app launches,
we'll likely want a thin banner at the top of the page with this if they're
willing- I think drip sequences may be a bit much for this audience, so really I
think from there were probably looking at basic monthly newsletter sends 

-   Thoughts on referral part of user journey- we should ask them if they plan
to offer referral links and incentivize them with a portion of revenue from the
users they refer. beyond that, apart from basic social referral campaigns, its 
hard to drive those types of referrals- I do think partnership referrals is a
big one - both from the standopoint  of crypto listing sites (defillama,
coingecko, coinmarketcap, etc), as well as other protocols that build into the
Nova protocol is important. this is probably a much wider conversation though

---
date: 2022-01-20
to: Darcie Tanner <darcie@makk.co>
subject: Re: Customer journey matrix & Strategy update
msg_id: 17e75ce42bc06bd1
---

This is an interesting point - it may be worth opening up maybe 20/30 minutes on
our next call with them to chat about how they see this.
From a channel standpoint, influencers are probably most impactful in the crypto
space on twitter, youtube, and maybe reddit, to a lesser extent discord.
Some influenncers  are pay  to play, others won't accept payment, and it's very
difficult to know which is which. Fees can be quite astronomical. I also do know
an agency founder who runs an influencer outreach service, but charges $15k
minimum, one-off and likely 6 figure annual subscription, but he has sold out
quite a few sets of Solana NFTs using this (and bills between a 5 and 8x ROI
basically garunteed). His strategy is very scrape/automated mass outreach
though, so I do think there's a more minimalist/scalable approach to this
There's actually been a bit of a backlash against anonymous influencers over the
past week or so that's been interesting, so I could see the Nova
founders having strong opinions on using or not using paid influencers. that
being said, we can always do organic outreach campaigns, offer interviews/AMAs,
etc
In terms of focus areas for me, these generally make sense to me - a good mix of
some of the more technical tradtionalmarketng  areas I'mgood and  are neglected
in crypto + a fair amount of crypto specific stuff. Is there anything you want
me to do to flesh these out immediately? I'm a bit packed through the weekend,
but should likely be able to put a few bullets together on each of those points
by Monday or Tuesday of next week
The other area to consider also might be partnerships. Given the
interrelated/build-on-top-of-each-other nature of a lot of these largely open
source projects, and the need for things like the ability to swap tokens in
various places and in various pairs, there's a lot of interreliance of founders
building on a specific chain, so we'll likely want to do as much as possible to
use cross promotion to try and tap their audiennces
Similarly we should be heavily targeting any
influencers/news/media/accounts/sites that are oriented around Solana

Week of the 24th is very flexible for me - I’m on the west coast, but are

more ideal)?

people in that group are looking to go.

Also, are you going to BTC Miami next week? Know a few people there who
might be interesting to connect with.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2022-01-15
to: Jennifer Furr <jbfurr@umich.edu>
subject: Very random but…
msg_id: 17e5c2c341351c39
---

Hi Jennifer,

Hope all is well with you!

I thought you’d find it interesting that I actually worked in a few music
tech companies a few years after graduating - your audio production classes
actually were very useful for me in those :)

I’ve been trying to find in old course documents and by googling, but can’t
for the life of me find that track you played for us one day in class that
was sampling tarot cards and chips and a shaman talking - us there any
chance you could send it to me or tell me what it is? It’s been nagging at
me for weeks now.

Thanks again for teaching one of my favourite classes in college :)

Matthew

date: 2022-01-13
to: Susan Amat <Susan@venturehive.com>
subject: Re: Hi Dan!
msg_id: 17e55ac3f415c821
---

That'd be great - and let me know sometime in the coming week or two if you'd
like to jump on a call more generally.
For some context, I work a fair bit setting up investment and accelerator
programs for Eterna Capital, Algorand, Oasis/Rose, etc, and various other
crypto/blockchain ecosystem growth projects. Previously did a lot of growth and
data consulting with startups, the startup journey a few times, and ran
marketing and data across Startupbootcamp for a few years.
But in the process of jumping full time onto https://techtree.dev to lead our
web3/token sale from existing web2 product.

---
date: 2022-01-13
to: ross@zest.capital, pawel@techtree.dev
subject: Pawel/Ross Intro
msg_id: 17e557cb08335a4f
---

Hi Ross,
Please meet Pawel, a long time mentor and collaborator, and the CEO of TechTree.
He can do a bit better of a job describing our product and how we could
potentially help you with hiring/align otherwise. He also has a very interesting
background in investment and advisory, and likely would have some interesting
connections for you.
Pawel, please meet Ross, who was in the Algorand accelerator I ran and was one
of 2 companies I put money in to  afterwards. He's building infrastructure tools
for DAOs on Algorand with some solid backing, and looking to do some technical
hiring as well.
I'll let you two find a time and potentially join to wallflower  if the timing
works for me on the west coast USA.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-01-07
to: Pat Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
subject: Re: GameStop Entering NFT, Cryptocurrency Markets as Part of Turnaround - The Wall Street Journal.
msg_id: 17e35d6591f48b75
---

My immediate thoughts around this were to laugh hysterically and that it is
total BS, just a money grab to capitalize on their retail traction
I finally actually read the article though, and not that there was anything
explicitly in there that made me think they will do this, but it got my brain
jogging on what they could actually do that might be interesting - I could
actually see there being consumer interest and value in
movie/film/entertainment-oriented digital asset marketplace. An future end point
for this could even be to sell NFTs that represent future royalties for super
fans as a crowdfunding mechanism (especially for more indie studios), which is
sort of already being done in music by a company I've done some work with,
Opulous:
https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/republic-is-bringing-music-investing-to-the-masses-with-artists-lil-pump--kshmr-through-security-nfts-with-opulous-301393994.html
. But there's clearly some demand for this with the recent Matrix and Space Jam
and other entertainment NFT drops. But I do think 90% of these brand drops will
be done inscencerily as money grabs, which I do not think will hold up long term
in this space.
But more simply, even just a curated marketplace of digital movie merchandise
(and even maybe a distribution point for films themselves - see the Mila Kunis
and Ashton Kutcher NFT/TV show Stoner Cats) could be very interesting to
consumers potentially if they can get multiple studios on it. I could also see
there being interesting cross-overs between something like NFT movie
distribution and the Black Mirror Bandersnatch choose your own adventure thing,
where different NFTs unlock different story paths, and maybe some other more
functionally unique use cases/customer experiences via that.
But all of this is totally speculative, and this could completely be a money
grab.

---
date: 2022-01-07
to: mukaiss@webblen.com
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17e33c82ab42512a
---

Hey Kai,

Was great to catch up the other week. Just following up on a few points:

1) Let me know if there’s any way to OTC buy some Webblen tokens soon :)

2) I have a call with a partner at Eterna Capital next week, and will lay
some groundwork to intro you. If yiu get a lead investor for you next
round, let me know and there’s someone else there I’ll intro you to who
manages their more formal deal flow

3) I’ve attached some info about TechTree. I’d love to pick your brain on
it a bit more as we bring it to market, and I’m sure there might be some
useful bouncing of knowledge off each other between our tech lead and you.
If you’re looking to do some engineering hires, we’d love to help as well,
and potentially explore some events around technical topics and hiring

4) would you be interested in connecting with the founder from smile
coin/grin gaming? He pinged me today asking if I knew some people working
on Algorand smart contracts, so I introed him to our tech lead at TechTree,
but maybe also useful for you as well?

5) I mentioned Ivan from Social Coin who does some interesting work using
tokens to drive community and corporate engagement, long before blockchain.
And we used him when I was a researcher at Berkeley Haas to do some
experiments (could also potentially intro you to those professors if you
wanted to facilitate research using data from your platform). Happy to try
and make a connection -
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ivancaballero

And of course, let me know if there’s anything else I can help with or if I
can get more involved with what you’re working on :) there are a few other
potential investors I can think of that might be quite interesting for you
(although somewhat moonshot approaches - Naval and Niv from Angellist,
former CTO of eventbrite, etc)), and always up to help with go to market
planning and execution. But I’m general would love to continue our
conversation and dive a bit deeper into what you’re focused on.

And do let me know if there’s anything we talked about I forgot here.

Best,

Matthew




About TechTree:



A social network where developers help each other to level up while getting
rewarded for the help they offer. Initially, we’re helping engineers to
help each other find other devs to work with and getting paid the same
amount of money as recruiters for it (over 10k USD per successful
recommendation). We have already paid our users over 300k USD in bounties
for such activities with the biggest single hire being worth 45k USD.

Our partners and clients are some of the biggest VCs and tech companies in
the world like: Seedcamp, Local Globe, Northzone or Personio, Tessian,
Advent, and many more.

We’re backed by some amazing investors like Frontline Ventures, Notion
Capital, and fantastic angels including ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday,
senior execs from Stackoverflow, Uber, Monzo and many more. We’re a 15
people team doing slightly over 50k USD monthly revenue.

Here’s some more information about us:


   1.

   Deck <https://brieflink.com/v/bazah>
   2.

   Light paper:
   <https://www.notion.so/techtreedev/Request-for-funding-TechTree-1fb248c8640f45199e9b4f16664a4123>

   3.

   Blog - why we decided to build TechTree:
   <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1c5BaA39vY8i8DbkXQl63wfxU0MlGs95-dEJyVWb5Wlw/edit#>

date: 2022-01-07
to: Pat Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
subject: Re: Catching up
msg_id: 17e3364fa6577f1d

Hi Pat,

The Hirst book is called the 12 Million Dollar Stuffed Shark if it’s of
interest, although I have to say the writing style is slow and dry and
taking me longer than I’d like to get through it - but it’s a good
conceptualization of the economics of contemporary art.

Sorry for the delay on my side - I got a bit wrapped up in the holidays and
with some last minute family travel. Hope your holiday was lovely and you
got to spend time with the family.

It’d be great to connect with a few people on your team. Feel free to intro
me to whoever might be interested/ing. And would be great to find another
time for a Zoom - let me know when might work for you.

I’ll also likely be in SF on Tuesday and Wednesday of this week, and for
another week later in the month, if you might be around.

Hope you new year is off to a good start!

Matthew

date: 2022-01-06

subject: Re: Fwd: TechTree Token overview

Hey man - sorry I actually only just opened this - crazy holidays. And hope your
new year is off to a great start!

Best,

---
date: 2022-01-06
to: Jason Whitt <whitt.j.b@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Good meeting
msg_id: 17e2d60f9606d655
---

Hey Jason,
Was great to meet yesterday.
Following up on a few points:
1) This is the equity free accelerator for people building on ROSE/Oasis that
could maybe be of interest -https://oasisaccelerate.com/
2) Let me know if you'd like me to try and connect you with this designer who's
worked for a number of blockchain protocols and applications (and was a
cofounder of a company LocallyApproved, which sounds similar to the target
market you're addressing) -https://jonathansaffari.com/
3) I've pasted a bit more information about what we're working on at TechTree
(still very early stages). Let me know if you'd like me to keep you in the loop
as we move to raise and launch our token, and if there's anyone you think might
be interesting for us to connect with, that be fantastic.
And of course, let me know if there's anything else!
Best,
Matthew




About TechTree:A social network where developers help each other to level up
while getting rewarded for the help they offer. Initially, we’re helping
engineers to help each other find other devs to work with and getting paid the
same amount of money as recruiters for it (over 10k USD per successful
recommendation). We have already paid our users over 300k USD in bounties for
such activities with the biggest single hire being worth 45k USD. 

Our partners and clients are some of the biggest VCs and tech companies in the
world like: Seedcamp, Local Globe, Northzone or Personio, Tessian, Advent, and
many more.
We’re backed by some amazing investors like Frontline Ventures, Notion Capital,
and fantastic angels including ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday, senior execs
from Stackoverflow, Uber, Monzo and many more. We’re a 15 people  team doing
slightly over 50k USD monthly revenue.
Here’s some more information about us:
 1. Deck
    
    
 2. Light paper:
    
    
 3. Blog - why we decided to build TechTree:

work for you?
Looking forward to it!
Matthew

---

ack sorry forgot my investor list I promised you - very preliminary but will be

---
date: 2022-01-06
to: nick@gringaming.com
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17e2d489e4156b62
---

Hey Nick,
Was great to catch up yesterday - really enjoyed it, let me know if you want to
kick it again soon.
Following up on a few points we chatted on -
1) I had a quick chat with our tech lead (who is ex-Fabric Ventures and has been
building blockchain for 6 years or so, but mainly Eth and Sol), and he would be
keen to chat with you if you might be open to it just to validate some of the
approaches he's taking towards our smart contract and token development if you
might be open to it
2) I'm catching up with Nassim from Eterna Capital next week (he also just
joined the discord - un: nasco), so will  mention to him then what  you're
working on and offer an intro. They typically do DD for investments through the
fund once there's a lead for the round on, so if you have one I can also intro
you to Mattia who manages their pipeline, but they also do some investment
outside the fund with quite a bit more flexibility (which I believe is how they
backed Tinyman)
3) If there are any other connections I mentioned that might be useful, do let
me know and more  than happy to make them. I'd also love to connect with that
consumer tech fund you mentioned and anyone else you might think would be of
interest.
4) For a SMILE OTC buy, can I do $10k USD? Let me know what that looks like or
if there's anything I should consider there.
5) I've attached a bit of info about TechTree  below. If you might be interested
in using the platform (or just otherwise learning more and connecting with
someone interesting), I can intro you to our CEO Pawel  - he's a serial
entrepreneur and probably raised 5-10 A rounds for various startups in crypto
and web2 tech, so might have some other interesting contacts for you too.

Let me know if there's anything I'm forgetting, and let me know if there's
anything I can help you with!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2022-01-04
to: Jeff Kelley <jeff@mainchainventures.com>
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 17e2656a2390b8c7
---

Hi Jeff,
Hope your new year is off to a good start!
First, I just wanted to thank you for our conversation last summer. It got me
much deeper into NFTs, which has been quite good timing (especially coupled with
the fact that most of my NFT plays in 2020 that I did make were metaverse
properties). Also slightly embarrassed on my FTMPunks call for you, although
I've actually had some of my biggest wins in crypto with FTM chain NFTs.
I've actually moved back stateside, mainly in LA, but bouncing around quite a
bit. I also recently jumped off my consulting day job full time into web3
working with some friends of mine I had previously invested in to build an
engineering community/talent platform (sort of Braintrust + communities/social
network with heavy referral hiring incentive schemes, and some solid early VC
backers/users and revenue). I'd love to pick your brain on it sometime as we
plan the token sale and bring it to market, particularly as my knowledge of the
US environment is a little weak compared to more international. I should be in
Florida in the coming month or two, but would love to catch up before then if
you might have some time.
I also have recently started a discord group with one of my friends, and we've
built it up to 80 or so people (some very interesting backgrounds, 4-5 web3
founders, engineers/senior mgmt at most of the big tech companies, lots of
startup employees, etcetc, although it is a big degenerate too). Today someone
posted about the NFT LA event, and I saw you were a sponsor on it. I'd love to
catch up if/when you're here, and if you might have a discount on some tickets,
I think quite a few friends from the discord group are keen to go :)
And if the discord is interesting to you at all, here's a
link: https://discord.gg/djE29fMw6B
Hope to catch up soon, and that all is well!
Best,
Matthew
